proposals.title: Higher-Order Quantum Corrections to Effective D-Brane Charges in Type II String Compactifications
proposals.motivation: The quantization of D-brane charges receives quantum corrections that shift the allowed charge lattice, as famously demonstrated by the Freed-Witten anomaly and the half-unit shift for D0-brane charge in the presence of background flux. While the leading-order shift has been understood through K-theory and index-theoretic arguments, systematic computation of higher-order corrections remains incomplete, leaving gaps in our understanding of the full quantum-corrected charge lattice and its implications for the Swampland program and flux compactification landscape counting.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that higher-order quantum corrections to effective D-brane charges, arising from higher-derivative couplings on the brane worldvolume and bulk alpha-prime corrections, produce a systematically computable tower of sub-leading shifts that modify the charge lattice structure in ways constrained by the Swampland conjectures, particularly the Completeness Hypothesis and the Distance Conjecture.
proposals.method: We will compute higher-order corrections to D-brane charges using three complementary techniques. First, we extend the Chern-Simons coupling on D-brane worldvolumes to include higher-derivative terms involving the A-roof genus and its gravitational corrections at order alpha-prime squared and beyond, extracting the induced lower-dimensional brane charges via anomaly inflow. Second, we employ the Atiyah-Singer index theorem on the internal compactification manifold to compute the full index of the Dirac operator twisted by gauge and tangent bundles, capturing topological corrections at all orders. Third, we cross-check results using string amplitude computations for boundary states in exactly solvable CFT backgrounds such as Gepner models and toroidal orbifolds, where exact worldsheet methods allow verification of the effective field theory predictions.
proposals.contribution: This work will produce the first systematic catalog of sub-leading quantum corrections to D-brane charges through order alpha-prime cubed in Type IIA and IIB compactifications on Calabi-Yau threefolds. The results will refine flux vacuum counting in the string landscape by correcting the tadpole cancellation conditions, directly impacting estimates relevant to Vafa's Swampland program. Additionally, the corrected charge lattice will provide new consistency checks for the Completeness Hypothesis.
proposals.novelty: While individual pieces—anomaly inflow, index theory, boundary state computations—are well established, no prior work has systematically combined all three to extract the full tower of sub-leading charge shifts and connected these corrections to Swampland constraints. The interplay between higher-derivative worldvolume couplings and the Swampland Completeness Hypothesis at sub-leading order is entirely unexplored.

proposals.title: S-Duality Partition Functions for Non-Simply-Connected Gauge Groups: Discrete Theta Angles and Swampland Constraints
proposals.motivation: Most tests of S-duality in four-dimensional N=4 super-Yang-Mills theory assume simply-connected gauge groups such as SU(N), yet the global form of the gauge group and associated discrete theta angles have profound consequences for the partition function's modular properties. Following the clarification by Aharony, Seiberg, and Tachikawa that a complete specification of a gauge theory requires choosing a global form (e.g., SU(N)/Z_k) together with discrete topological parameters, a systematic study of how these choices modify S-duality tests remains incomplete. This gap is especially pressing within Vafa's Swampland program, where precise duality constraints distinguish consistent quantum gravity vacua from the swampland.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that non-simply-connected gauge groups with nontrivial discrete theta angles produce a richer modular structure in the Vafa-Witten partition function, where S-duality acts as a permutation on the space of global form and discrete theta parameter choices, and that this extended modular structure yields new Swampland-type constraints on which global gauge group structures can arise in consistent string compactifications.
proposals.method: We will compute the Vafa-Witten twisted partition function on compact four-manifolds (CP^2, K3, and Hirzebruch surfaces) for gauge groups SU(N)/Z_k with all inequivalent discrete theta angles classified by H^2(M, Z_k). The partition functions will be organized into vector-valued modular forms, and we will determine the explicit SL(2,Z) representation matrices that mix different discrete theta sectors under S and T transformations. We will employ the Langlands dual group correspondence to predict how S-duality maps between different global forms, cross-checking against known results for SO(3) versus SU(2). Finally, we will interface these constraints with the Swampland Completeness Hypothesis to determine whether all global forms mandated by modular covariance are realized in known string compactifications via F-theory and M-theory constructions.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic classification of S-duality transformation rules for Vafa-Witten partition functions across all non-simply-connected forms of classical gauge groups with discrete theta angles. It will generate concrete new Swampland constraints linking global gauge group topology to consistent UV completion, directly extending Vafa's program. The results will also clarify which discrete theta angle configurations appear in F-theory compactifications on elliptically fibered Calabi-Yau manifolds with nontrivial Tate-Shafarevich groups.
proposals.novelty: While individual cases (e.g., SO(3) vs SU(2)) have been studied, no unified framework treats all global forms, discrete theta angles, and their modular mixing simultaneously, nor has this structure been systematically connected to Swampland constraints. The interplay between discrete theta angles and the Completeness Hypothesis provides a novel bridge between number-theoretic modularity and quantum gravity consistency conditions.

proposals.title: Quantum Geometry Corrections to M-Theory Vacua: Beyond the Large Calabi-Yau Volume Limit
proposals.motivation: Most computations of effective theories from M-theory compactifications rely on the large volume limit of the internal Calabi-Yau manifold, where classical geometry is a reliable approximation. However, the string landscape includes many vacua where the internal volume is of order the string scale, precisely where quantum geometric effects—worldsheet instantons, higher-derivative corrections, and non-perturbative contributions—become dominant. Understanding whether the vacuum structure and symmetry properties identified by Vafa and collaborators in recent work on M-theory-like vacua persist at small volumes is critical for assessing the robustness of swampland constraints and the landscape of consistent quantum gravity theories.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that key structural features of four-dimensional M-theory vacua, including gauge symmetry enhancements and moduli stabilization patterns, are qualitatively modified at small Calabi-Yau volumes due to quantum geometry corrections, and that certain vacua permitted in the large-radius approximation are in fact inconsistent when the full quantum geometry is incorporated, thereby sharpening swampland criteria.
proposals.method: We will systematically compute worldsheet instanton corrections and genus-one topological string free energies for a representative class of compact Calabi-Yau threefolds using mirror symmetry and the holomorphic anomaly equations of Bershadsky-Cecotti-Ooguri-Vafa. These quantum corrections will be incorporated into the four-dimensional effective supergravity action obtained from M-theory compactification. We will then analyze the corrected scalar potential and vacuum structure using numerical minimization and analytic expansion techniques, comparing against the large-volume results. Special attention will be paid to enhanced symmetry loci in moduli space where quantum corrections are expected to be most significant, leveraging the classification framework from Vafa's recent work on symmetries and M-theory-like vacua.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic study of how quantum geometry modifies the vacuum landscape of M-theory compactifications at small internal volumes. It will either validate the robustness of existing swampland conjectures in the quantum regime or identify concrete modifications needed, directly informing the Elementary Constituents Conjecture and the broader swampland program. The results will establish quantitative benchmarks for when the large-volume approximation breaks down.
proposals.novelty: While quantum corrections to individual Calabi-Yau compactifications have been studied, no systematic analysis has connected these corrections to the swampland program and the classification of M-theory vacua in four dimensions. This proposal uniquely bridges the exact results of topological string theory with the structural vacuum analysis pioneered in Vafa's recent work, probing a regime that has been assumed away rather than confronted.

proposals.title: Systematic Extraction of Integer BPS Invariants from Topological String Partition Functions via Refined Stable Pair Theory
proposals.motivation: Topological string partition functions encode BPS degeneracies through the Gopakumar-Vafa formula, yet extracting the underlying integer invariants remains plagued by ambiguities in the multi-covering contributions and regularization of divergent sums. A rigorous, systematic framework would unify disparate computational methods across compact and non-compact Calabi-Yau geometries, directly impacting Vafa's Swampland program by sharpening the constraints that BPS spectra impose on consistent quantum gravity vacua. Resolving these extraction ambiguities is essential for testing conjectures such as the Weak Gravity Conjecture at the level of exact degeneracies.
proposals.hypothesis: A refined stable pair formalism, combined with wall-crossing structure from Kontsevich-Soibelman theory, provides a canonical and algorithmically computable decomposition of the topological string free energy into integer BPS invariants at all genera and curve classes, eliminating the multi-cover ambiguities present in current Gopakumar-Vafa extractions.
proposals.method: We reformulate the Gopakumar-Vafa expansion using Pandharipande-Thomas stable pair invariants, which are rigorously defined via virtual fundamental classes and naturally produce integer-valued counts. We then incorporate the Kontsevich-Soibelman wall-crossing formula to track how BPS indices jump across moduli space, establishing a canonical chamber where the invariants are unambiguously defined. Computationally, we implement a recursive algorithm exploiting the finite-product structure of the refined partition function in the Nekrasov-Shatashvili limit, extracting spin-refined BPS indices genus-by-genus for toric Calabi-Yau threefolds using the refined topological vertex. We validate the framework against known results for local del Pezzo surfaces and the quintic threefold, and extend to geometries relevant to the quasicrystalline string landscape recently studied by Vafa.
proposals.contribution: This work delivers a mathematically rigorous pipeline from topological string amplitudes to integer BPS invariants, resolving long-standing extraction ambiguities and providing open-source computational tools for the string theory community. The refined invariants yield new constraints on BPS spectra relevant to Swampland conjectures, and the framework generalizes naturally to the motivic and categorical refinements needed for M-theory lifts.
proposals.novelty: While Gopakumar-Vafa integrality has been assumed since the late 1990s and stable pair theory provides mathematical rigor for genus-zero cases, no systematic computational framework unifies refined wall-crossing with stable pair theory across all genera. Our approach is the first to combine Kontsevich-Soibelman structures with the refined topological vertex into a single extraction algorithm, yielding provably integer outputs without ad hoc regularization.

proposals.title: Schwinger Amplitudes for Non-Isolated Holomorphic Curves and Coincident M2-Brane Bound States in M-Theory Compactifications
proposals.motivation: In M-theory compactifications on Calabi-Yau fourfolds, BPS states arising from M2 branes wrapping holomorphic curves play a central role in determining the low-energy effective action and the structure of the Swampland. While isolated rational curves yield well-understood contributions to superpotentials and particle spectra, non-isolated curves—those possessing continuous moduli—give rise to richer physical effects that remain poorly characterized. Understanding the Schwinger-type pair-production amplitudes for multiple coincident M2 branes on such curves is essential for extracting the full non-perturbative content of M-theory vacua and for testing Vafa's Swampland conjectures in settings with enhanced gauge symmetry.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the Schwinger pair-production amplitude for k coincident M2 branes wrapping a non-isolated holomorphic curve of genus g with an m-dimensional moduli space factorizes into a moduli-space integral over a determinantal structure governed by the normal bundle cohomology, yielding corrections to the effective action that scale non-trivially with both k and m and that are constrained by the Weak Gravity Conjecture.
proposals.method: We will first classify families of non-isolated holomorphic curves in compact Calabi-Yau fourfolds relevant to four-dimensional N=1 M-theory vacua, computing their normal bundle deformation spaces using sheaf cohomology and spectral sequence techniques. For each family, we will derive the one-loop Schwinger integrand by evaluating the functional determinant of the M2-brane worldvolume action expanded around the classical embedding, incorporating the zero-mode integration over the curve moduli space. For multiple coincident branes, we will employ the non-abelian M2-brane effective action (ABJM-type) to compute the k-brane determinant, tracking how the SU(k) gauge field on the worldvolume modifies the spectral asymmetry and index density. Finally, we will perform modular integration of the resulting amplitudes and extract their implications for the effective superpotential, comparing with existing topological string and Gopakumar-Vafa invariant calculations in limiting cases.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic computation of Schwinger amplitudes for non-isolated curves with continuous moduli in M-theory, extending the Gopakumar-Vafa framework beyond isolated curve contributions. It will establish how multi-brane bound states on families of curves generate new non-perturbative corrections to the four-dimensional effective action, providing concrete tests of the Swampland Distance Conjecture and the Weak Gravity Conjecture in compactifications with rich curve moduli.
proposals.novelty: Previous calculations of M2-brane contributions have focused almost exclusively on isolated curves or have treated non-isolated curves only at the level of counting invariants without computing full Schwinger amplitudes. The combination of continuous moduli integration with the non-abelian multi-brane determinant structure is technically new and reveals qualitatively different scaling behavior that has no analog in the isolated-curve setting.

proposals.title: Exact M2-Brane Amplitudes from Equivariant Localization on K3 via Type IIA Reduction
proposals.motivation: Computing exact nonperturbative amplitudes in M-theory remains one of the central open problems in string theory, as direct path-integral methods for M2-branes lack the controlled saddle-point structure available in lower-dimensional gauge theories. The M-theory/Type IIA duality on K3 surfaces offers a uniquely tractable arena because K3 compactifications preserve half-maximal supersymmetry and their BPS spectra are tightly constrained by established mathematical invariants such as Gromov-Witten and Gopakumar-Vafa invariants. Leveraging this duality to import equivariant localization techniques from topological field theory into the M2-brane sector would bridge a longstanding gap between formal localization results and genuine M-theoretic observables.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the equivariant supercharge and torus-action parameters governing M2-brane localization on an eleven-dimensional background can be consistently transported through the M-theory/Type IIA reduction on K3, such that the exact M2 path integral reduces to a calculable K3 sigma-model amplitude whose saddle points are in one-to-one correspondence with holomorphic curve moduli and sheaf-theoretic data already classified by topological string theory.
proposals.method: We will begin by formulating the equivariant cohomological structure of the M2-brane worldvolume theory on backgrounds of the form X7 × K3, identifying the relevant supercharge Q and the U(1) equivariant parameters compatible with the K3 isometry group. We then perform a systematic dimensional reduction to Type IIA on K3 and map Q-fixed loci to BPS configurations described by stable sheaves and holomorphic curves on K3, using the Atiyah-Bott fixed-point theorem adapted to the reduced moduli space. The one-loop determinants around each saddle will be computed via index-theoretic methods—specifically the elliptic genus of the K3 sigma model and its refinements—and matched against known Gopakumar-Vafa and Noether-Lefschetz invariants. Finally, we will reconstruct the eleven-dimensional measure factors by inverting the duality map, providing an explicit formula for the exact M2 partition function on the chosen background.
proposals.contribution: This work would deliver the first concrete computation of an exact M2-brane amplitude derived from equivariant localization through string duality, establishing K3 as a calibration geometry for nonperturbative M-theory calculations. It would clarify which localization saddle points survive the duality and how their measure factors are organized, providing a blueprint extensible to more general Calabi-Yau and G2 backgrounds. The results would also furnish new consistency checks on the Swampland program by connecting exact amplitude data to constraints on effective field theories descending from M-theory.
proposals.novelty: While equivariant localization is well-developed in supersymmetric gauge theories and topological strings separately, no prior work has systematically transported equivariant parameters through M-theory/Type IIA duality to extract exact M2-brane path integrals. The use of K3 not merely as a compactification manifold but as a calibration space that filters localization saddle points via established topological invariants is a conceptually new strategy that connects Vafa's Swampland insights with modern mathematical tools in enumerative geometry.

proposals.title: Magnetic Gravitational Couplings from Wrapped M5-Branes: A Dual Formulation of the Swampland Distance Conjecture
proposals.motivation: The Swampland program, pioneered by Vafa and collaborators, constrains effective field theories that can be consistently coupled to quantum gravity. Magnetic gravitational couplings have been studied primarily through M2-brane configurations, but the electromagnetic duality structure of M-theory suggests that wrapped M5-branes should encode complementary and potentially stronger constraints. Understanding this dual formulation is essential for completing the web of Swampland conjectures and for probing the non-perturbative structure of string compactifications.
proposals.hypothesis: Wrapped M5-branes on calibrated cycles of internal manifolds generate magnetic duals of the gravitational couplings computed from M2-branes, and these dual couplings impose independent constraints on the low-energy effective theory that refine existing Swampland bounds on the scalar field space geometry.
proposals.method: We compactify M-theory on Calabi-Yau fourfolds and G2 manifolds, wrapping M5-branes on coassociative four-cycles and divisors to compute magnetic gravitational couplings via anomaly inflow and the M5-brane worldvolume action including the self-dual two-form. We derive the effective four-dimensional couplings by integrating the six-dimensional (2,0) tensor multiplet theory over the wrapped cycles, tracking how the resulting terms transform under electromagnetic duality relative to the known M2-brane electric couplings. Topological data of the cycles—intersection numbers, characteristic classes, and calibration conditions—are used to express the magnetic couplings in closed form. We then compare these results against the Swampland Distance Conjecture and the Weak Gravity Conjecture to extract new bounds on moduli space distances and gauge coupling hierarchies.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic derivation of magnetic gravitational couplings from wrapped M5-branes, completing the electric-magnetic duality picture for gravitational Swampland constraints. The resulting bounds are expected to sharpen existing distance conjecture decay rates and may reveal new tower scales invisible to M2-brane analyses. These results strengthen the case for the universality of Swampland constraints across dual frames.
proposals.novelty: While M2-brane contributions to gravitational couplings are well-studied, the M5-brane magnetic dual has not been systematically formulated due to the technical complexity of the self-dual tensor field on the M5-brane worldvolume. Our approach leverages recent advances in anomaly inflow for M5-branes and the classification of calibrated cycles to make this computation tractable for the first time.

proposals.title: Non-Kodaira Fibers and Exotic Gauge Algebras in F-theory: Obstructions and Realizations Beyond ADE
proposals.motivation: In F-theory compactifications, gauge symmetries arise from singular elliptic fibers classified by Kodaira and Néron. This classification maps directly onto ADE-type Lie algebras, and essentially all known F-theory gauge sectors fall within this framework. However, recent work on non-geometric string backgrounds, frozen singularities, and the Swampland program—particularly Vafa's constraints from the Elementary Constituents Conjecture and quasicrystalline landscapes—raises the question of whether the Kodaira catalog truly exhausts all consistent gauge structures in quantum gravity, or whether exotic fibers could extend the landscape of allowed gauge symmetries.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that certain non-Kodaira singular fibers, realized through non-flat resolutions or T-brane data on elliptically fibered Calabi-Yau manifolds, can give rise to gauge algebras that do not embed into the standard ADE classification, and that Swampland consistency conditions sharply constrain which such exotic gauge sectors can appear in consistent quantum gravity vacua.
proposals.method: We will systematically classify singular fibers on elliptic Calabi-Yau threefolds and fourfolds that fall outside the Kodaira-Néron table, using techniques from Weierstrass model analysis and explicit toric resolutions. Specifically, we will construct non-flat fiber degenerations over complex codimension-one and codimension-two loci, computing their resolved intersection structures to extract the associated gauge algebras via the Shioda-Tate-Wazir theorem generalized to non-flat settings. We will then incorporate T-brane backgrounds—non-abelian Higgs field configurations with nilpotent residues—to identify cases where the low-energy gauge algebra departs from ADE type. Finally, each candidate exotic gauge sector will be tested against Swampland constraints including the Completeness Conjecture, the absence of global symmetries, and anomaly cancellation in six and four dimensions.
proposals.contribution: This work would either identify the first concrete examples of non-ADE gauge algebras in consistent F-theory vacua or establish a no-go theorem proving that Swampland principles enforce ADE exclusivity. Either outcome significantly sharpens our understanding of the string landscape and provides new evidence for or against the completeness of the Kodaira classification as a physical constraint rather than merely a mathematical convenience.
proposals.novelty: While non-Kodaira fibers have been noted sporadically in the mathematics literature, no systematic study has combined their explicit construction with T-brane deformations and Swampland filtering. Connecting fiber geometry beyond ADE to Vafa's recent conjectures on elementary constituents and vacuum structure provides a novel bridge between algebraic geometry and quantum gravity constraints.

proposals.title: Non-Adiabatic F-Theory/Heterotic Duality in Six Dimensions: Direct Matching of Moduli Spaces and Anomaly Polynomials
proposals.motivation: F-theory/heterotic duality in six dimensions has historically been established by fibering the well-understood eight-dimensional duality over a common base, invoking an adiabatic argument that assumes fiber volumes remain large. This assumption breaks down in physically interesting regimes where the fiber shrinks, precisely where novel strongly-coupled phenomena such as tensionless strings and enhanced gauge symmetries emerge. A direct six-dimensional proof of the duality, bypassing the adiabatic limit, would place F-theory/heterotic correspondence on firmer footing and unlock access to non-perturbative corners of the string landscape.
proposals.hypothesis: F-theory/heterotic duality in six dimensions can be established directly by matching anomaly polynomials, moduli space geometries, and BPS state spectra on both sides without dimensional reduction from eight dimensions, and the resulting map reveals new dual pairs invisible to the adiabatic argument.
proposals.method: We construct explicit Calabi-Yau threefold elliptic fibrations with prescribed singular fibers encoding non-Abelian gauge algebras and match them to heterotic compactifications on K3 surfaces with specified instanton embeddings. The anomaly polynomial factorization on the F-theory side, computed via intersection theory on the resolved threefold, is directly compared to the one-loop heterotic result using the Green-Schwarz-Sagnotti mechanism. We systematically classify cases where the heterotic bundle data — SU(N) and Sp(k) instantons distributed across the two E8 factors with total instanton number 24 — yields identical tensor branch structures and hypermultiplet spectra. For each candidate dual pair, we verify matching of the prepotential on the tensor branch using techniques from Intriligator-Morrison-Seiberg and cross-check BPS string tensions against volumes of curves in the F-theory base.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic non-adiabatic verification of F-theory/heterotic duality in six dimensions across a broad class of models, including cases with multiple tensor multiplets and non-simply-laced gauge algebras where the adiabatic argument is unreliable. It yields a refined dictionary between geometric data of elliptic Calabi-Yau threefolds and heterotic K3 bundle data, and identifies new dual pairs that were missed by previous adiabatic constructions.
proposals.novelty: Existing treatments rely on the adiabatic fibering of the eight-dimensional duality, which obscures models where fiber moduli are frozen or strongly coupled. By working intrinsically in six dimensions using anomaly matching and moduli space geometry, we access dual pairs outside the adiabatic regime and provide independent evidence for the duality from BPS state counting rather than dimensional inheritance.

proposals.title: Inverse F-Theory: Algorithmic Reconstruction of Elliptic Calabi-Yau Threefolds from Gauge-Theoretic Data
proposals.motivation: F-theory compactifications on elliptic Calabi-Yau manifolds provide one of the richest frameworks for engineering gauge theories with prescribed matter content in string theory. While the forward map — from geometry to gauge group and matter spectrum — is well understood through the Kodaira classification of singular fibers and the Tate algorithm, the inverse problem of reconstructing the minimal geometry from a target gauge-theoretic specification remains unsolved. Solving this inverse problem would dramatically accelerate the systematic exploration of the string landscape and directly connect to Vafa's Swampland program by identifying which low-energy effective theories admit consistent UV completions.
proposals.hypothesis: For any gauge group G and matter representation R consistent with six-dimensional anomaly cancellation, there exists a constructive algorithm that produces the minimal elliptic Calabi-Yau threefold — specified by its base surface and Weierstrass data — realizing (G, R) via F-theory, and this reconstruction is unique up to birational equivalence when minimality is imposed.
proposals.method: We develop a three-stage inverse algorithm. First, we use anomaly cancellation constraints (gravitational, gauge, and mixed) to fix the topological data of the base surface, including its Hodge numbers and intersection form. Second, we apply the inverse Tate algorithm: given the target gauge algebra, we systematically reconstruct the orders of vanishing of the Weierstrass coefficients (f, g, Δ) along each divisor, working backward from the Kodaira fiber type dictionary. Third, we enforce matter spectrum constraints by tuning collision patterns of singular fibers at codimension-two loci, using the representation-theoretic decomposition tables of Katz, Morrison, Schafer-Nameki, and Sully. The algorithm is implemented as a constraint-satisfaction search over the Kreuzer-Skarke database of reflexive polytopes, pruned by the necessary conditions from the Swampland distance and weak gravity conjectures.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic inverse map from gauge-theoretic data to F-theory geometry, transforming what has been an art — guided by expert intuition — into a reproducible algorithmic procedure. It will produce a searchable catalog of minimal geometric realizations for all gauge groups up to rank 20 with charged matter, directly enabling tests of Swampland conjectures such as the completeness hypothesis and the elementary constituents conjecture proposed by Vafa. The framework also opens a computational pathway toward classifying the full set of consistent F-theory vacua in six and four dimensions.
proposals.novelty: While prior work has catalogued forward maps from geometry to physics extensively, no systematic inverse algorithm exists; existing constructions rely on case-by-case engineering. Our approach is non-obvious because it combines the algebraic constraints of the inverse Tate algorithm with global topological consistency conditions and Swampland bounds as simultaneous pruning criteria, reducing an a priori intractable search to a feasible constraint-satisfaction problem.

proposals.title: M-Theory Five-Brane Lifts of Surface Operator Defects and Non-Perturbative Matter Spectra in 4d N=1 Vacua
proposals.motivation: Surface operators in four-dimensional gauge theories encode rich non-perturbative data about the matter sector, yet their full stringy origin remains poorly understood. Vafa's recent work on M-theory-like vacua in four dimensions and the Elementary Constituents Conjecture highlights the need to access non-perturbative completions of the matter spectrum beyond perturbative string constructions. Lifting surface defect operators to M-theory five-branes offers a geometric route to capture these elusive contributions and constrain the allowed matter content from top-down considerations.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that codimension-two surface operator defects in Type IIA compactifications on Calabi-Yau threefolds admit a systematic lift to M5-brane configurations in M-theory, and that the resulting five-brane worldvolume theories encode non-perturbative matter sector data—including BPS spectra and flavor symmetry enhancements—that are invisible in the perturbative IIA description.
proposals.method: We will construct explicit M5-brane lifts of surface operators defined in IIA compactifications by identifying the appropriate special Lagrangian three-cycles wrapped by the five-branes in the G2-holonomy lift of the Calabi-Yau geometry. The worldvolume theory of the resulting M5-branes will be analyzed using the six-dimensional (2,0) tensor multiplet formalism, extracting the BPS spectrum via anomaly inflow and the M5-brane anomaly polynomial. We will compute the elliptic genus of the M5-brane worldvolume theory to enumerate non-perturbative states contributing to the four-dimensional matter sector, employing modular bootstrap techniques to constrain the spectrum. Finally, we will cross-check results against Swampland constraints, particularly the Elementary Constituents Conjecture, to verify consistency of the lifted matter content with quantum gravity expectations.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic dictionary between surface operator defects in four-dimensional N=1 gauge theories and M5-brane configurations, making non-perturbative matter sector contributions computationally accessible. It will yield new constraints on allowed matter representations in M-theory vacua and strengthen the bridge between the Swampland program and defect operator physics. The results will also furnish concrete predictions testable within the framework of topological string amplitudes.
proposals.novelty: While M5-brane lifts of line operators and domain walls have been extensively studied, the systematic lift of codimension-two surface operators—and the extraction of non-perturbative matter spectra from their worldvolume elliptic genera—has not been carried out. Combining this geometric lift with Swampland consistency conditions from the Elementary Constituents Conjecture provides a novel top-down filter on the matter sector that goes beyond existing anomaly-based methods.

proposals.title: Deformed SL(2,Z) Duality in F-Theory: Moduli-Dependent Breaking and Swampland Constraints
proposals.motivation: F-theory compactifications canonically assume that the type IIB axio-dilaton transforms under a rigid SL(2,Z) duality group, inherited from the exact S-duality of ten-dimensional type IIB string theory. However, in the presence of fluxes, warping, and quantum corrections in realistic compactifications, there is no guarantee that this full discrete symmetry survives intact at low energies. Understanding whether and how SL(2,Z) can be broken or deformed has profound implications for the landscape of effective field theories, the Swampland program, and the classification of consistent quantum gravity vacua.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that in F-theory compactifications with non-trivial flux backgrounds and higher-derivative corrections, the SL(2,Z) duality group is broken to finite-index subgroups or deformed into a quantum group structure, and that the pattern of breaking is constrained by Swampland conditions such as the Distance Conjecture and the absence of global symmetries.
proposals.method: We will systematically classify the possible breakings of SL(2,Z) to congruence subgroups Gamma_0(N), Gamma_1(N), and Gamma(N) induced by discrete flux quanta in F-theory on elliptically fibered Calabi-Yau fourfolds. Concretely, we will compute the monodromy group of the axio-dilaton around 7-brane stacks in the presence of G_4 flux using algebraic geometry techniques on the discriminant locus. We will then examine whether quantum group deformations of SL(2,Z) at roots of unity can arise from non-perturbative effects by analyzing the modular properties of the topological string partition function on these backgrounds. Finally, we will derive constraints on the allowed residual duality groups by imposing the Swampland Distance Conjecture on infinite-distance limits in the axio-dilaton moduli space.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic classification of SL(2,Z) breaking patterns in F-theory with fluxes, connecting the arithmetic structure of duality groups to physical Swampland constraints. It will establish new selection rules for consistent F-theory vacua beyond the standard assumption of full SL(2,Z) invariance, potentially narrowing the string landscape. The results will also clarify the role of modular symmetry in four-dimensional effective theories arising from string compactifications.
proposals.novelty: While partial SL(2,Z) breaking by monodromies around individual 7-branes is well known, the global breaking pattern induced by flux backgrounds and its interplay with Swampland conditions has not been systematically studied. The possibility of quantum group deformations of the duality group, rather than simple subgroup breaking, represents an entirely unexplored direction in F-theory model building.

proposals.title: Non-perturbative D-instanton Corrections to Type IIB Flux Compactifications from F-theory Elliptic Fibrations
proposals.motivation: Type IIB string theory compactified on Calabi-Yau orientifolds provides a rich framework for moduli stabilization and phenomenology, yet non-perturbative effects such as D-instanton contributions and strong coupling corrections remain poorly understood in the perturbative formulation. F-theory, which geometrizes the axio-dilaton as the complex structure of an elliptic fibration, naturally encodes these non-perturbative effects in the topology and geometry of the compactification manifold. Extracting these corrections systematically would close a significant gap in our understanding of the string landscape and moduli stabilization mechanisms like KKLT and the Large Volume Scenario.
proposals.hypothesis: Singular fibers of Kodaira type in F-theory elliptic Calabi-Yau fourfolds encode specific non-perturbative D-instanton and (p,q)-string bound state corrections to the type IIB superpotential that cannot be captured at any finite order in the string perturbation expansion, and these corrections modify the vacuum structure of flux compactifications in a classifiable manner.
proposals.method: We systematically classify singular elliptic fibrations over concrete base threefolds (such as P^3 and toric hypersurfaces) using Tate's algorithm and the Kodaira classification, mapping each singular fiber type to a specific non-perturbative sector in the type IIB limit. For each fibration, we compute the F-theory superpotential via period integrals on the Calabi-Yau fourfold using Picard-Fuchs differential equations and mirror symmetry techniques. We then take the Sen weak-coupling limit to extract the non-perturbative corrections order by order, comparing against known D-instanton calculus results where available. Finally, we incorporate background four-form flux (G_4) and use the tadpole cancellation condition to constrain the allowed non-perturbative sectors, determining how these corrections shift the positions of supersymmetric and metastable vacua.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide an explicit dictionary between F-theory geometric data and type IIB non-perturbative corrections for a broad class of compactifications, going beyond the handful of examples currently available in the literature. The resulting corrections to the scalar potential will quantitatively affect moduli stabilization, potentially altering the statistics of the flux landscape and the viability of specific de Sitter uplift mechanisms. This connects directly to Vafa's Swampland program by providing new tools to test which effective theories admit consistent UV completions.
proposals.novelty: While the F-theory/type IIB duality is well-established at the level of gauge symmetry and matter content, the systematic extraction of non-perturbative superpotential corrections from the geometry of singular fibers in the presence of G_4 flux has not been carried out for general fibrations. Our approach combines Picard-Fuchs period computations on fourfolds with the Sen limit in a unified computational framework, revealing corrections invisible to both perturbative string theory and standard D-instanton calculus.

proposals.title: Worldsheet CFT Methods for Classifying Non-Abelian Gauge Symmetry Enhancement at Singular Fibers in F-Theory Compactifications
proposals.motivation: F-theory compactifications on elliptically fibered Calabi-Yau manifolds realize non-abelian gauge symmetries through singular fibers classified by the Kodaira-Néron scheme. However, the precise mapping between the geometry of singular fibers and the emergent gauge algebra remains incomplete for non-minimal singularities and collisions of singular fibers in higher codimension. A systematic two-dimensional conformal field theory approach to these singularities would provide a unified framework connecting string worldsheet physics directly to the gauge-theoretic data encoded in the compactification geometry.
proposals.hypothesis: Two-dimensional conformal field theory techniques, specifically the representation theory of affine Lie algebras realized on the worldsheet near singular fibers, can systematically classify and predict gauge symmetry enhancement patterns at codimension-two and codimension-three singularities in F-theory, including cases not captured by the standard Kodaira classification.
proposals.method: We construct explicit two-dimensional CFTs associated with Type IIB string theory on ADE singularities by engineering N=(2,2) superconformal minimal models and their orbifolds corresponding to each Kodaira fiber type. Using the coset construction and branching rules for affine Lie algebras at specific levels, we compute the spectrum of massless states localized at singular fibers and their collisions. For codimension-two loci where fibers collide, we employ the fusion rules of the corresponding rational CFT to determine the enhanced gauge algebra, cross-checking against known Tate algorithm predictions. We extend the analysis to non-minimal singularities by studying logarithmic CFT deformations and non-unitary extensions, computing operator product expansions and modular invariant partition functions to extract the enhanced symmetry data.
proposals.contribution: This work provides a first-principles worldsheet derivation of gauge symmetry enhancement in F-theory, bridging the gap between the algebraic geometry of elliptic fibrations and the physical string spectrum. It extends the classification of singular fiber collisions beyond the standard Kodaira-Tate framework, potentially identifying new gauge enhancement patterns relevant to Vafa's Swampland program and constraints on consistent quantum gravity vacua.
proposals.novelty: While prior work has studied F-theory singularities primarily through algebraic geometry and M-theory duality, this proposal applies worldsheet CFT technology directly to the singular fibers, exploiting the representation-theoretic structure of affine algebras and modular invariance as independent classification tools that can detect enhancements invisible to purely geometric methods.

proposals.title: Reconstructing Knot Invariants from the Deformed Conifold: An Inverse Geometric Transition Approach
proposals.motivation: The Gopakumar-Vafa duality between Chern-Simons theory on the three-sphere and closed topological string theory on the resolved conifold has provided deep connections between knot invariants and Calabi-Yau geometry. However, the inverse problem—starting from the deformed conifold geometry and systematically recovering knot invariant data—remains largely unexplored. Solving this inverse problem would not only validate the geometric transition picture from a new direction but also reveal hidden structural constraints on knot invariants imposed by string duality.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the period integrals and flux data of the deformed conifold, combined with open-closed duality maps, uniquely determine the colored HOMFLY-PT polynomials of torus knots, and that the reconstruction procedure exposes a previously unrecognized integrality structure tied to BPS state counting via Gopakumar-Vafa invariants.
proposals.method: We begin by computing the full set of period integrals on the deformed conifold with flux, using Picard-Fuchs equations and their monodromies to extract the prepotential and its corrections order by order in the string coupling. We then apply the large-N geometric transition dictionary in reverse: the B-model closed string amplitudes on the deformed conifold are mapped to A-model open string amplitudes on the resolved conifold with Lagrangian branes, from which we extract knot invariant generating functions. Specifically, we use the topological vertex formalism and the reformulated invariants of Ooguri and Vafa to read off colored HOMFLY-PT polynomials for torus knots. Finally, we check the resulting invariants against known knot tables and analyze integrality properties predicted by the BPS expansion.
proposals.contribution: This work establishes a concrete computational pipeline for the inverse geometric transition, demonstrating that deformed conifold data suffices to reconstruct knot invariants without prior input from Chern-Simons theory. It provides new integrality checks on Gopakumar-Vafa invariants in the open string sector and may yield a geometric explanation for observed patterns in colored knot polynomials. The framework extends naturally to more general Calabi-Yau geometries relevant to Vafa's Swampland program.
proposals.novelty: While the forward direction of the Gopakumar-Vafa correspondence is well studied, no systematic reconstruction of knot invariants starting purely from the deformed conifold period geometry has been carried out. Our inverse approach reveals that the monodromy structure of the B-model periods encodes knot-theoretic data in a way that is invisible in the standard forward formulation.

proposals.title: Non-Perturbative Completion of Topological String Theory: Resurgence, Large-Order Behavior, and Trans-Series Structure of the Genus Expansion
proposals.motivation: The topological string genus expansion has been widely used to compute BPS invariants, black hole entropy, and exact superpotentials, yet the convergence properties of this expansion remain poorly understood. Evidence from matrix model dualities and specific Calabi-Yau backgrounds suggests the perturbative series is asymptotic and factorially divergent, analogous to field-theoretic perturbation theory. Establishing this rigorously and identifying the required non-perturbative sectors would reshape our understanding of topological string completeness and its physical predictions.
proposals.hypothesis: The topological string free energy genus expansion is generically asymptotic with factorial growth of coefficients governed by instanton actions, and its full non-perturbative completion takes the form of a resurgent trans-series where Borel singularities correspond to D-brane or NS-brane instantons wrapping specific cycles of the target Calabi-Yau.
proposals.method: We will compute high-genus free energies (up to genus ~50-100) on select compact and non-compact Calabi-Yau threefolds using the holomorphic anomaly equations and direct integration, leveraging modularity constraints to fix ambiguities. The large-order behavior of these coefficients will be analyzed via Richardson extrapolation and Borel-Padé techniques to extract the leading singulant (instanton action) and Stokes constants. We will then construct explicit trans-series ansätze incorporating non-perturbative sectors and verify resurgent relations—namely that lateral Borel resummations across Stokes lines reproduce the correct discontinuities predicted by the trans-series. Finally, we compare extracted instanton actions against known D-brane tensions and central charges computed from special geometry.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic demonstration of resurgence in topological string theory across multiple Calabi-Yau geometries, establishing the precise non-perturbative sectors required for a complete definition. It connects abstract mathematical resurgence machinery to physical brane instantons, offering a new computational window into non-perturbative string effects and potentially constraining the Swampland program's completeness conjectures.
proposals.novelty: While resurgence has been explored in matrix models and simple quantum mechanical systems, a comprehensive genus-by-genus analysis directly in topological string theory on compact Calabi-Yau manifolds—connecting Borel singularities to geometric brane data—has not been carried out, making this a genuinely new bridge between resurgent asymptotics and string geometry.

proposals.title: Reconstructing Calabi-Yau Geometry from Topological String Amplitudes via the Holomorphic Anomaly Inverse Map
proposals.motivation: Topological string theory computes amplitudes order-by-order in the genus expansion, encoding rich geometric data of the target Calabi-Yau threefold. While the forward problem—computing amplitudes from a known geometry via mirror symmetry and the holomorphic anomaly equations of Bershadsky-Cecotti-Ooguri-Vafa (BCOV)—is well-established, the inverse problem of systematically recovering the Calabi-Yau moduli space geometry and mirror map from amplitude data remains open. Solving this inverse problem would provide a powerful new handle on the string landscape and connect Vafa's Swampland program to concrete geometric classification.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the full genus expansion of topological string free energies, subject to BCOV holomorphic anomaly equations and appropriate gap/regularity conditions at singular loci, uniquely determines the underlying Calabi-Yau moduli space geometry, including the prepotential, Kähler potential, and mirror map, up to discrete symmetry equivalences.
proposals.method: We will develop an algorithmic inverse framework operating in three stages. First, from the genus-zero and genus-one free energies, we extract the classical prepotential and Yukawa couplings via their known relation to special geometry, reconstructing the periods and mirror map using Picard-Fuchs differential equations. Second, we exploit the recursive structure of the BCOV holomorphic anomaly equations at higher genus: treating the propagators and holomorphic ambiguities as unknowns, we impose boundary conditions at conifold, orbifold, and large-radius points—using gap conditions established by Huang-Klemm-Quackenbush—to solve for the moduli-space connection data. Third, we employ computational algebraic geometry tools (Griffiths-Dwork reduction, GKZ hypergeometric systems) to reconstruct candidate Calabi-Yau geometries whose periods match the extracted data, checking consistency against the genus expansion at each stage. We will test the method on one- and two-parameter models (the quintic, hypergeometric families) where forward computations provide validation benchmarks.
proposals.contribution: This work would establish the first systematic algorithm for the topological string inverse problem, demonstrating that amplitude data can serve as a geometric fingerprint for Calabi-Yau manifolds. It provides a new tool for classifying geometries relevant to string compactification and offers a concrete bridge between Swampland constraints on effective theories and the underlying geometric landscape.
proposals.novelty: While individual pieces—BCOV recursion, gap conditions, period reconstruction—exist separately, no unified inverse pipeline has been constructed. Our approach is non-obvious because it requires proving that the holomorphic ambiguities, traditionally fixed by forward-problem boundary data, can themselves be bootstrapped purely from amplitude-side consistency conditions without prior geometric knowledge.

proposals.title: Pinning Down the Swampland Distance Conjecture Constant from Flux Compactifications and Quasicrystalline Landscapes
proposals.motivation: The Swampland Distance Conjecture asserts that an infinite tower of states becomes exponentially light as one traverses a geodesic distance Δφ in moduli space, with the decay rate governed by a universal constant c of order one. Despite its centrality to the swampland program, the precise value of c remains undetermined, weakening the conjecture's predictive power for phenomenology and cosmology. Sharpening this bound would directly constrain large-field inflation models, dark energy quintessence scenarios, and the structure of the string landscape.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that a systematic survey of tower mass spectra across diverse string compactification geometries—including Calabi-Yau threefolds, G2 manifolds, and the recently proposed quasicrystalline string landscapes—reveals a sharp universal lower bound c ≥ 1/√(d−2) in d dimensions, with the bound saturated by decompactification limits in M-theory vacua.
proposals.method: We will compute the exponential decay rate of the lightest tower of states along every infinite-distance geodesic in the moduli spaces of Type IIA/IIB flux compactifications on complete-intersection Calabi-Yau manifolds, leveraging existing databases of topological data (e.g., the Kreuzer-Skarke list). For each geometry, we extract the mass scale of KK towers, winding modes, and emergent string towers as functions of the saxionic moduli, using dimensional reduction and one-loop determinants. We extend this analysis to four-dimensional M-theory-like vacua with discrete symmetries, as studied by Vafa and collaborators, and to quasicrystalline compactification lattices where the non-periodic tiling structure modifies the standard KK spectrum. Statistical regression over O(10⁴) infinite-distance limits will identify the distribution of c values and its infimum.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first large-scale empirical determination of the swampland distance constant c from explicit string constructions, converting a qualitative order-one estimate into a precise, falsifiable bound. The result would immediately sharpen constraints on trans-Planckian field excursions relevant to axion monodromy inflation and quintessence models, and would test whether quasicrystalline landscapes obey or tighten the standard conjecture.
proposals.novelty: While individual examples of tower decay rates have been computed, no systematic statistical survey across thousands of geometries has been attempted. Incorporating quasicrystalline string landscapes—where aperiodic lattice structure could modify the tower spectrum in unexpected ways—is entirely novel and connects Vafa's recent work on quasicrystalline compactifications directly to quantitative swampland bounds.

proposals.title: S-Duality and the Swampland Distance Conjecture: Mapping Gradient Bounds from Weak to Strong String Coupling
proposals.motivation: The Swampland Distance Conjecture and its refined variants impose gradient bounds on scalar field potentials in effective theories consistent with quantum gravity, yet these constraints have been studied predominantly at weak string coupling. S-duality, a well-established symmetry of Type IIB string theory exchanging weak and strong coupling, provides a powerful non-perturbative lens through which to examine whether such bounds are preserved, modified, or sharpened. Understanding how Swampland constraints transform under S-duality would illuminate the deep structure of the string landscape and constrain the space of consistent quantum gravity vacua.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that S-duality maps the de Sitter gradient bound of the refined Swampland Distance Conjecture to a dual constraint at strong string coupling that is at least as restrictive, and that the combined weak-strong duality web yields a tighter, self-consistent bound on scalar potentials in the full non-perturbative theory.
proposals.method: We will work within the framework of Type IIB string theory compactified on Calabi-Yau threefolds, exploiting the SL(2,Z) S-duality group acting on the axio-dilaton. First, we derive the explicit transformation of the scalar potential gradient bound under SL(2,Z), tracking how the tower of light states required by the Distance Conjecture maps between perturbative string excitations and D-string or (p,q)-string towers. Second, we analyze specific flux compactification examples using the Gukov-Vafa-Witten superpotential to compute the dual gradient constraints at strong coupling numerically. Third, we extend the analysis to four-dimensional N=2 settings connected to Vafa's recent work on M-theory-like vacua, checking consistency with the Elementary Constituents Conjecture.
proposals.contribution: This work would establish the first systematic dictionary between Swampland gradient bounds at weak and strong coupling, providing new non-perturbative evidence for the refined Distance Conjecture. It would also produce concrete predictions for how the tower mass decay rate transforms under S-duality, offering testable constraints for model builders working in strongly coupled string vacua.
proposals.novelty: While S-duality and the Swampland program have each been extensively studied, a systematic mapping of Swampland gradient bounds through the full SL(2,Z) duality group has not been carried out. The approach of using strong-coupling dual constraints to sharpen weak-coupling Swampland bounds is a novel strategy that leverages non-perturbative self-consistency in a way previous analyses have not exploited.

proposals.title: Tropical Limits of Topological String Amplitudes and Integrable Hierarchy Solutions via Combinatorial Degeneration
proposals.motivation: Topological string theory on Calabi-Yau threefolds generates partition functions that satisfy integrable hierarchies such as the Toda and KP equations, yet explicit closed-form solutions remain elusive beyond low-genus or special geometries. Tropical geometry, which replaces algebraic curves with piecewise-linear graphs, has proven powerful for computing Gromov-Witten invariants through correspondence theorems pioneered by Mikhalkin. Connecting tropical degeneration directly to the integrable structure of topological string amplitudes would open a combinatorial route to exact solutions in regimes inaccessible by conventional mirror symmetry techniques.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the tropical limit of the spectral curve underlying topological string theory on toric Calabi-Yau threefolds yields piecewise-linear tau-function solutions to the associated integrable hierarchies, and that these tropical tau-functions reproduce the full genus expansion via a systematic refinement procedure governed by the combinatorics of tropical curve counting.
proposals.method: We begin by constructing the tropical degeneration of spectral curves for the resolved conifold and local del Pezzo geometries, obtaining tropical spectral networks whose edge weights encode period integrals. We then apply the topological recursion of Eynard-Orantin on tropical curves, leveraging the discrete residue calculus developed by Gathmann and Markwig, to compute genus-by-genus free energies. These tropical free energies are assembled into a combinatorial tau-function, whose satisfaction of Hirota bilinear equations for the Toda lattice hierarchy is verified order by order. Finally, we employ the Gross-Siebert program's wall-crossing formalism to systematically reconstruct the full algebraic tau-function from its tropical skeleton, establishing an exact correspondence between tropical curve counts and integrable hierarchy solutions.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first explicit dictionary between tropical geometric data and tau-function solutions of integrable hierarchies arising from topological strings. It offers a purely combinatorial algorithm for computing topological string amplitudes to arbitrary genus on toric backgrounds, circumventing the computational bottlenecks of holomorphic anomaly equations. The framework also creates new connections between the Gross-Siebert mirror symmetry program and the integrability structure of string theory.
proposals.novelty: While tropical geometry and integrable hierarchies have each been studied extensively in the context of topological strings, no prior work has directly constructed tau-function solutions from tropical spectral curve data or used wall-crossing to reconstruct the full integrable structure from its tropical limit. This synthesis is non-obvious because the piecewise-linear degeneration typically destroys the complex-analytic structure on which standard integrability proofs rely.

proposals.title: A-Model Integrable Hierarchies via Gromov-Witten Theory: Mirror Dual Formulation of Toda and KP Structures
proposals.motivation: Mirror symmetry establishes deep correspondences between A-model and B-model topological string theories, yet the integrable hierarchies governing partition functions have been predominantly understood from the B-model perspective through matrix models and special geometry. The celebrated results of Dijkgraaf-Vafa and earlier work by Eguchi-Yang connecting B-model amplitudes to Toda and KP hierarchies lack a fully intrinsic A-model counterpart, leaving a conceptual gap in our understanding of why integrability arises in topological string theory. Constructing the dual integrable hierarchy directly in A-model language would illuminate the geometric origin of these structures and potentially unlock new computational tools for enumerative geometry.
proposals.hypothesis: The integrable hierarchy governing the B-model partition function possesses a mirror dual formulation expressible entirely in terms of genus-zero and higher-genus Gromov-Witten invariants of the A-model target space, where the hierarchy's tau-function is reconstructed from the quantum cohomology ring and its gravitational descendants without passage through mirror map inversion.
proposals.method: We will begin by studying toric Calabi-Yau threefolds where both A-model Gromov-Witten theory and B-model matrix model descriptions are well-established, using the topological vertex formalism to express partition functions combinatorially. We then identify the Lax operators and Hamiltonian densities of the known B-side integrable hierarchy and translate them through the mirror map into A-model quantum cohomology operations, exploiting Givental's formalism and the Dubrovin connection to express flows as shifts in Novikov variables. The Virasoro constraints and W-algebra symmetries of the A-model will be analyzed to verify closure of the hierarchy, employing localization techniques on moduli spaces of stable maps to compute the requisite correlators. Finally, we extend the construction beyond the toric setting using wall-crossing formulae and the Gopakumar-Vafa BPS invariant reformulation to test universality.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first self-contained A-model derivation of integrable hierarchies for topological string partition functions, eliminating the need to invoke mirror symmetry as an intermediary. It would yield new recursion relations among Gromov-Witten invariants organized by integrable flows, offering efficient computational algorithms for higher-genus enumerative invariants. The framework would also clarify how Vafa's swampland constraints and string landscape structure interface with integrability in the topological sector.
proposals.novelty: While Givental and Dubrovin established connections between quantum cohomology and integrable systems at genus zero, extending this to an all-genus integrable hierarchy intrinsically on the A-model side—without invoking the mirror B-model—has not been achieved. Our approach of reverse-engineering the Lax representation through the topological vertex and then abstracting beyond toric geometries via BPS state counting constitutes a fundamentally new strategy.

proposals.title: Spin-Refined Topological Vertex: Disentangling BPS Spin Content via an Additional Equivariant Parameter
proposals.motivation: The topological vertex formalism of Aganagic, Klemm, Mariño, and Vafa provides a powerful combinatorial tool for computing BPS invariants of toric Calabi-Yau threefolds, yet the standard refined vertex conflates left and right spin quantum numbers into two equivariant parameters without cleanly separating individual spin multiplet contributions. Vafa's program connecting string compactifications to the Swampland and BPS state counting demands finer-grained invariants that track spin content explicitly, especially as refined BPS indices play a central role in checks of string dualities and wall-crossing phenomena. A vertex formalism that distinguishes spin representations would sharpen these tests and open new connections to gauge-theoretic enumerative invariants.
proposals.hypothesis: By introducing a third equivariant parameter that grades BPS states according to their SU(2)_L spin representation independently of the existing (q,t) refinement, one can define a spin-refined topological vertex whose partition functions decompose refined BPS invariants into explicit spin multiplet contributions, yielding new integrality constraints and reproducing known motivic Donaldson-Thomas invariants as a specialization.
proposals.method: We extend the refined topological vertex of Iqbal, Kozçaz, and Vafa by augmenting the Macdonald-polynomial basis with an additional fugacity z coupled to the Cartan generator of SU(2)_L, constructing a three-parameter vertex amplitude C_{λμν}(q,t,z). The gluing rules along internal edges of the toric diagram are modified to include a z-dependent propagator derived from the equivariant index of the resolved conifold with spin grading. We verify the formalism by computing the partition function of local P^2 and the resolved conifold, checking that the z→1 limit recovers the standard refined vertex and that expansion coefficients satisfy the integrality predicted by motivic Donaldson-Thomas theory. Wall-crossing behavior is analyzed by varying Kähler moduli and comparing spin-graded BPS spectra against predictions from the Kontsevich-Soibelman formula.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first explicit combinatorial vertex formalism that fully disentangles spin content of refined BPS invariants for arbitrary toric Calabi-Yau threefolds. It yields new integrality predictions testable against independent geometric computations and establishes a direct bridge between the topological vertex program and motivic Donaldson-Thomas theory. The enhanced invariants also furnish sharper consistency checks for Swampland conjectures that constrain BPS spectra in quantum gravity.
proposals.novelty: While the refined topological vertex introduced a second equivariant parameter beyond the original Gopakumar-Vafa formulation, no existing framework provides a closed-form vertex amplitude that individually resolves spin multiplet contributions. The introduction of a third grading parameter and the corresponding modified gluing rules constitute a non-trivial extension requiring new combinatorial identities for triple-Macdonald-type functions that have not previously appeared in the literature.

proposals.title: Planar Resummation and Non-Perturbative Dynamics in Non-Supersymmetric Gauge Theories: Lessons from the Swampland
proposals.motivation: Large-N planar diagram resummation has proven remarkably powerful in extracting non-perturbative information from N=1 and N=2 supersymmetric gauge theories, yet its applicability to non-supersymmetric theories remains largely unexplored. Understanding whether planar resummation can capture confinement, chiral symmetry breaking, and other non-perturbative phenomena without supersymmetry would bridge a critical gap between formal string-theoretic methods and realistic QCD-like theories. Vafa's Swampland program and recent work on non-supersymmetric string vacua provide new constraints that may guide which non-supersymmetric theories admit tractable planar limits.
proposals.hypothesis: Planar diagram resummation in specific non-supersymmetric gauge theories—selected by Swampland consistency conditions and proximity to supersymmetric parents via soft breaking—captures key non-perturbative observables such as the string tension and chiral condensate, with accuracy controlled by the degree of supersymmetry breaking.
proposals.method: We begin by identifying candidate non-supersymmetric gauge theories that satisfy Swampland constraints, particularly those arising from orientifold projections of N=1 parents where supersymmetry is broken by boundary conditions or anti-brane sectors. For each candidate, we systematically resum the planar Feynman diagrams using Schwinger-Dyson equations in the large-N limit, implementing the loop equations on a discretized lattice to handle infrared divergences. We compare the resummed results against lattice Monte Carlo simulations for SU(N) Yang-Mills with adjoint fermions at varying masses, interpolating between the supersymmetric and purely bosonic limits. Finally, we employ the refined topological vertex and matrix model techniques developed in the context of topological string theory to organize the planar expansion and identify which non-perturbative sectors survive the breaking of supersymmetry.
proposals.contribution: This work will establish quantitative criteria for when planar resummation remains a reliable tool beyond supersymmetry, directly connecting Swampland consistency conditions to the tractability of non-perturbative gauge dynamics. It will produce concrete predictions for the string tension and condensates in non-supersymmetric large-N theories testable against existing lattice data. The results will inform the broader program of identifying consistent non-supersymmetric vacua in string theory by clarifying which low-energy dynamics can be reliably computed.
proposals.novelty: While planar resummation and Swampland constraints have been developed independently, using Swampland criteria to select non-supersymmetric theories where planar methods remain predictive is a novel synthesis. The interpolation strategy—continuously deforming from supersymmetric to non-supersymmetric regimes while tracking the breakdown of planar accuracy—has not been systematically pursued despite the maturity of both large-N and soft-breaking techniques.

proposals.title: Swampland Bounds from Pure Field Theory: Proving the Weak Gravity Conjecture Without String Compactification
proposals.motivation: The Swampland program, largely pioneered by Vafa and collaborators, seeks to delineate which effective field theories can be consistently coupled to quantum gravity. Most existing proofs of Swampland conjectures—particularly the Weak Gravity Conjecture (WGC)—rely on explicit string theory embeddings or holographic arguments, leaving open whether these constraints are truly universal or artifacts of the string lamppost. A purely field-theoretic derivation would establish these bounds as inevitable consequences of any consistent UV completion containing gravity, dramatically strengthening the Swampland program's foundations.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the Weak Gravity Conjecture and related Swampland bounds can be derived from consistency conditions intrinsic to quantum field theory coupled to gravity—specifically from unitarity, causality, and analyticity of gravitational scattering amplitudes—without requiring embedding into string theory or invoking holography.
proposals.method: We will employ the modern S-matrix bootstrap for gravitational EFTs, extending dispersive sum rules for graviton-photon and graviton-scalar scattering amplitudes to extract two-sided bounds on charge-to-mass ratios. Concretely, we will construct crossing-symmetric dispersion relations for four-point amplitudes involving U(1) gauge bosons and gravitons, imposing positivity constraints from the optical theorem and Regge boundedness. By systematically exploring the space of allowed Wilson coefficients using semidefinite programming (implemented via SDPB), we will identify regions where no unitary, causal UV completion exists unless superextremal states are present. We will further incorporate higher-dimensional operator constraints from the Elementary Constituents Conjecture to sharpen bounds in theories with multiple gauge groups and varying spacetime dimensions.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first rigorous, string-independent proof of the Weak Gravity Conjecture from field-theoretic axioms alone, validating a central pillar of the Swampland program on purely bottom-up grounds. It would also yield new numerical bounds on EFT coefficients in gravitational theories, directly testable against known string compactifications. The framework developed here would serve as a template for bootstrapping other Swampland conjectures beyond their current string-theoretic derivations.
proposals.novelty: While dispersive arguments have been applied to non-gravitational EFTs and partial gravitational bounds exist, no complete derivation of the WGC from pure S-matrix consistency has been achieved. Our approach uniquely combines the gravitational bootstrap with insights from Vafa's Elementary Constituents Conjecture to handle the forward-limit subtleties specific to gravity, closing a gap that has resisted resolution since the conjecture's formulation in 2006.

proposals.title: Topological Recursion for Higher-Genus Superpotential Corrections in Flux Compactifications
proposals.motivation: Exact superpotential computations in string compactifications are essential for moduli stabilization and vacuum selection, yet higher-genus corrections remain largely inaccessible beyond one loop. Matrix model descriptions of certain N=1 gauge theories and their string duals encode these corrections in principle, but systematic extraction at arbitrary genus has not been carried out. Given Vafa's program connecting the string landscape to swampland constraints, a recursive computational framework for these corrections would sharpen the boundary between consistent and inconsistent low-energy effective theories.
proposals.hypothesis: The Eynard-Orantin topological recursion applied to the spectral curve of the Dijkgraaf-Vafa matrix model computes all-genus superpotential corrections for the dual flux compactification, and the resulting asymptotic series encodes non-perturbative information about vacuum stability consistent with swampland conjectures.
proposals.method: We begin by identifying the spectral curve of the matrix model dual to Type IIB flux compactifications on local Calabi-Yau geometries with N=1 supersymmetry, following the Dijkgraaf-Vafa correspondence. We then apply the Eynard-Orantin topological recursion to compute the free energies F_g and multi-resolvent correlators W_{g,n} up to genus five or higher, extracting the effective superpotential order by order in the 1/N expansion. The recursion kernels are constructed from the Bergman kernel on the spectral curve, and we verify integrality and modularity properties of the coefficients against known geometric invariants. Finally, we perform Borel resummation of the genus expansion to probe non-perturbative corrections and compare with instanton contributions predicted by the landscape structure.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first systematic higher-genus superpotential corrections for flux vacua using topological recursion, yielding explicit analytic expressions at each genus. It would establish a concrete computational bridge between matrix model techniques and the swampland program, enabling quantitative tests of conjectures such as the de Sitter conjecture at the perturbative level. The results would also produce new enumerative invariants for the relevant Calabi-Yau geometries.
proposals.novelty: While topological recursion has been extensively developed for topological string theory and Hermitian matrix models, its systematic application to superpotential corrections in physically relevant N=1 flux compactifications—and the connection of its resurgent structure to swampland bounds—has not been explored. This bridges the formal mathematical machinery of Eynard-Orantin with Vafa's physical landscape and swampland programs in a previously unexploited way.

proposals.title: Swampland Constraints and Large-N Dualities Beyond SU(N): Testing the String Landscape Correspondence for SO(N) and Exceptional Gauge Groups
proposals.motivation: The Swampland program, to which Vafa has been a central contributor, has largely been tested in the context of SU(N) gauge theories where the 't Hooft large-N expansion provides a well-understood bridge to string theory via gauge/gravity duality. However, SO(N) and exceptional gauge groups (G2, F4, E6, E7, E8) possess qualitatively different 't Hooft expansion structures—featuring both orientable and non-orientable ribbon graphs—raising the question of whether Swampland constraints such as the Weak Gravity Conjecture and the Distance Conjecture hold uniformly across these sectors. Clarifying this is essential for determining whether the Swampland criteria are truly universal or artifacts of the SU(N) large-N limit.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that Swampland constraints derived from SU(N) gauge/string correspondences require non-trivial modifications when extended to SO(N) and exceptional gauge groups, and that the differing topological structure of their large-N expansions leads to distinct spectra of light towers near infinite-distance limits in moduli space.
proposals.method: We will systematically construct the large-N expansion for SO(N) gauge theories on compact manifolds, tracking both orientable and non-orientable worldsheet contributions, and compare the resulting string duals with those of SU(N). For exceptional groups, where no standard large-N limit exists, we will use F-theory compactifications on elliptically fibered Calabi-Yau manifolds with the corresponding gauge symmetry singularities to extract the effective low-energy spectrum. We will then test the Weak Gravity Conjecture and Distance Conjecture by computing BPS state spectra and scalar field distances in moduli space for each gauge group using established techniques from topological string theory and spectral network analysis. Finally, we will compare the tower of states predicted near infinite-distance points across all gauge groups to identify universal versus group-dependent features.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic test of Swampland universality beyond the SU(N) sector, either confirming that the conjectures are gauge-group-independent or identifying precise corrections needed for orthogonal and exceptional groups. The results will sharpen the boundary between the string landscape and the swampland and may reveal new consistency conditions for quantum gravity that are invisible in the SU(N) limit.
