proposals.novelty: While prior Swampland tests have focused almost exclusively on SU(N) theories with standard 't Hooft ribbons, no systematic analysis exists for gauge groups whose large-N topology includes crosscaps and whose exceptional rank structure precludes a conventional planar limit. This proposal fills that gap by combining F-theory geometry with non-orientable worldsheet analysis in a unified framework.

proposals.title: Large-N Matrix Models for Chern-Simons Partition Functions and Emergent Dual Calabi-Yau Geometries
proposals.motivation: Chern-Simons gauge theories on three-manifolds encode deep topological and geometric information, yet exact computation of their partition functions at large rank remains a central challenge in mathematical physics. The Gopakumar-Vafa duality relating Chern-Simons theory on the three-sphere to closed topological strings on the resolved conifold demonstrated that large-N transitions can reveal hidden dual geometries. Extending this program to more general three-manifolds and gauge groups could uncover new open/closed string dualities and constrain the string landscape, connecting directly to Vafa's Swampland program and his recent work on quasicrystalline structures in the landscape.
proposals.hypothesis: Systematic application of random matrix model techniques to Chern-Simons partition functions on Seifert manifolds and lens spaces will reveal previously unknown large-N dual Calabi-Yau geometries, and the resulting free energies will satisfy integrality constraints consistent with BPS state counting governed by Gopakumar-Vafa invariants.
proposals.method: We reformulate Chern-Simons partition functions on Seifert manifolds as multi-matrix integrals using the surgery representation and Weyl integration formula, reducing the path integral to eigenvalue models with logarithmic and polynomial potentials. We solve these matrix models at large N via saddle-point analysis and the loop equation/topological recursion formalism of Eynard-Orantin, extracting the planar free energy and genus-by-genus corrections. The resulting spectral curves are interpreted as mirrors of candidate dual Calabi-Yau threefolds, whose geometry we reconstruct using techniques from special geometry and periods of holomorphic three-forms. We systematically check that the all-genus free energies admit an expansion in terms of integer Gopakumar-Vafa invariants, providing a non-trivial consistency condition on the proposed dualities.
proposals.contribution: This work will produce a catalogue of new large-N dualities between Chern-Simons theories on Seifert manifolds and closed topological string theories on explicit Calabi-Yau backgrounds, substantially extending the Gopakumar-Vafa correspondence beyond the conifold. It will provide new computational tools for extracting enumerative invariants from matrix models and generate concrete predictions testable against localization results. The identified dual geometries will offer new data points for constraining consistent string vacua within the Swampland program.
proposals.novelty: While individual Chern-Simons matrix models have been studied, no systematic classification of their large-N spectral curves as mirrors of dual Calabi-Yau geometries exists for general Seifert manifolds. Our approach uniquely combines Eynard-Orantin topological recursion with Swampland consistency conditions to both discover and constrain new string dualities simultaneously.

proposals.title: Gauge/Geometry Duality as Derived Category Equivalence: A Homological Mirror Symmetry Approach to Swampland Constraints
proposals.motivation: The gauge/geometry duality underlying string compactifications remains formulated in physical language that obscures its deep mathematical structure, limiting our ability to systematically classify consistent vacua. Kontsevich's homological mirror symmetry (HMS) program has matured into a powerful categorical framework connecting symplectic and algebraic geometry, yet its implications for the Swampland program and Vafa's constraints on effective field theories have not been systematically explored. A categorical reformulation would provide rigorous, computable invariants to distinguish landscape from swampland vacua.
proposals.hypothesis: The gauge/geometry duality in string compactifications on Calabi-Yau manifolds can be precisely reformulated as an equivalence between the derived category of coherent sheaves on the algebraic side and the Fukaya category on the symplectic side, and this equivalence imposes categorical obstructions that reproduce known Swampland conjectures—particularly the Weak Gravity Conjecture and the Distance Conjecture—as constraints on the stability conditions of objects in these categories.
proposals.method: We will construct explicit derived category equivalences for a class of Calabi-Yau threefolds relevant to Vafa's recent work on string landscape classification, focusing on cases where the gauge theory data (bundles, branes) maps to geometric data (cycles, special Lagrangians). Using Bridgeland stability conditions on the derived category of coherent sheaves, we will identify walls of marginal stability and translate them into physical constraints on gauge couplings and scalar field distances. Computational verification will proceed via direct calculation of Ext groups and A-infinity structures on specific toric Calabi-Yau examples where both the Fukaya category and the derived category are tractable. We will then systematically check whether the categorical obstructions to extending equivalences beyond certain moduli space regions reproduce the Distance Conjecture's exponential mass decay.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first systematic categorical derivation of Swampland constraints from homological mirror symmetry, bridging two major programs in mathematical physics. It would yield new computable invariants—categorical entropy and Bridgeland stability walls—as diagnostic tools for identifying consistent string vacua. The framework would also generate concrete predictions for which gauge theories admit UV completion, testable against known string constructions.
proposals.novelty: While HMS and the Swampland program have developed independently, this proposal uniquely leverages Bridgeland stability conditions as a mathematical mechanism to derive physical consistency constraints, an approach not explored in existing literature. The categorical perspective transforms qualitative Swampland conjectures into quantitative obstructions computable from the homological algebra of brane categories.

proposals.title: Two-Dimensional Gauge Theory as a Testing Ground for Swampland Dualities and Geometry Correspondences
proposals.motivation: The Swampland program, pioneered by Vafa and collaborators, seeks to distinguish consistent quantum gravity vacua from the vast landscape of effective field theories. Many conjectured dualities between gauge theories and geometric structures remain difficult to verify in higher-dimensional settings due to computational complexity and the lack of exact results. A two-dimensional analog would provide a controlled environment where exact techniques—such as localization, topological field theory methods, and integrable structures—can be brought to bear, offering concrete tests of duality mechanisms that underpin the Swampland constraints.
proposals.hypothesis: A suitably constructed two-dimensional gauge theory with appropriate matter content and topological sectors exhibits a precise gauge/geometry correspondence that mirrors the higher-dimensional duality mechanisms central to the Swampland program, and this correspondence can be verified exactly using localization techniques, thereby providing a lower-dimensional proof of concept for conjectured Swampland dualities.
proposals.method: We construct families of two-dimensional N=(2,2) gauged linear sigma models (GLSMs) whose infrared phases map to distinct geometric targets, including Calabi-Yau manifolds and their mirrors, following the framework of Witten and Morrison-Plesser but extended to incorporate Swampland-motivated constraints. We compute exact partition functions on S^2 using supersymmetric localization à la Benini-Cremonesi and Doroud-Gomis-Le Floch-Lee, extracting the central charge, Kähler potential, and BPS spectrum as functions of Fayet-Iliopoulos parameters. We then systematically identify which regions of the FI parameter space yield theories consistent with Swampland conjectures—specifically the Distance Conjecture and the Weak Gravity Conjecture—by tracking the behavior of the tower of BPS states near boundaries of moduli space. Finally, we compare these exact two-dimensional results against predictions from dimensional reduction of Vafa's four-dimensional constructions involving M-theory-like vacua and quasicrystalline lattice structures.
proposals.contribution: This work establishes a rigorous, exactly solvable testing ground for Swampland duality conjectures, providing the first systematic verification of gauge/geometry correspondences in a setting where all quantities are computable. It creates a dictionary between two-dimensional GLSM data and Swampland constraints that can guide the formulation of sharper conjectures in higher dimensions. The results also clarify the role of discrete symmetries and quasicrystalline structures in the string landscape from a bottom-up, lower-dimensional perspective.
proposals.novelty: While two-dimensional GLSMs and Swampland conjectures are individually well-studied, their systematic intersection—using exact localization results to test and refine Swampland bounds—has not been explored. This approach is non-obvious because the Swampland program has traditionally focused on four- and higher-dimensional effective theories, and demonstrating that its constraints leave meaningful imprints in two dimensions requires identifying the correct observables and duality frames.

proposals.title: Reconstructing Gauge Theories from Resolved Calabi-Yau Geometries via the Inverse Gopakumar-Vafa Correspondence
proposals.motivation: The Gopakumar-Vafa duality establishes that closed topological string amplitudes on resolved conifold geometries can be rewritten as 't Hooft large-N expansions of Chern-Simons gauge theories. While the forward direction—computing string amplitudes from a known gauge theory—is well developed, the inverse problem of starting from an arbitrary resolved Calabi-Yau threefold and systematically identifying the dual gauge theory remains open. Solving this inverse problem would provide a powerful classification tool for the string landscape and deepen our understanding of gauge/geometry duality beyond the conifold.
proposals.hypothesis: For any resolved Calabi-Yau threefold whose topological string free energy admits a 't Hooft-like large-N expansion, the dual gauge theory can be algorithmically reconstructed from the geometry's compact divisor structure, Gopakumar-Vafa invariants, and the pattern of Kähler moduli controlling the resolution.
proposals.method: We will develop a systematic algorithm that takes as input the triple intersection numbers, genus-zero and higher-genus Gopakumar-Vafa invariants, and the Kähler cone structure of a resolved Calabi-Yau threefold. First, we extract the 't Hooft expansion coefficients by reorganizing the topological string free energy in powers of a candidate string coupling and 't Hooft parameter, using the integrality structure of GV invariants as a constraint. Second, we classify the resulting expansion against known matrix model and Chern-Simons partition functions on Seifert manifolds, employing spectral curve reconstruction techniques from the Eynard-Orantin topological recursion framework. Third, we apply this algorithm to families of local Calabi-Yau geometries—including resolved A_n singularities fibered over curves and local del Pezzo surfaces—where partial gauge theory duals are known, to validate and extend the correspondence. Finally, we investigate obstructions: geometries whose GV invariants do not assemble into a consistent large-N expansion, characterizing when the inverse map fails.
proposals.contribution: This work would establish the first systematic inverse map from Calabi-Yau geometry to gauge theory within the Gopakumar-Vafa framework, transforming an existence question into a constructive algorithm. It would yield new gauge theory duals for geometries beyond the conifold and its immediate generalizations, and provide concrete criteria for when such duals exist, informing Vafa's Swampland program by distinguishing geometries with consistent gauge theory completions.
proposals.novelty: Previous work has addressed specific examples of geometric transitions case by case; this proposal reframes the problem as an algorithmic inverse map using the integrality of GV invariants and topological recursion as the key structural constraints. The systematic obstruction analysis—determining which geometries lack gauge duals—is entirely new and connects to Swampland criteria in a previously unexplored way.

proposals.title: Chiral Rings and Spectral Flow in N=2 Sigma Models with Non-Compact Target Spaces
proposals.motivation: Chiral rings are central algebraic structures in N=2 superconformal field theories, encoding protected operator algebras that govern topological subsectors and connect to geometric invariants of target spaces. While the theory of chiral rings is well-developed for compact Calabi-Yau target spaces, non-compact geometries—such as those arising in local Calabi-Yau models, conifold resolutions, and the Swampland program—remain poorly understood due to continuous spectra and subtleties in spectral flow. Given Vafa's foundational contributions to chiral rings and the growing importance of non-compact geometries in string landscape and Swampland analyses, a systematic extension is both timely and necessary.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that chiral rings for N=2 sigma models on non-compact target spaces admit a well-defined algebraic structure after appropriate regularization of the continuous spectrum, and that spectral flow maps between Ramond and Neveu-Schwarz sectors can be extended to these settings by introducing boundary data at asymptotic infinity that selects a discrete, normalizable subsector of the ring.
proposals.method: We will construct explicit N=2 superconformal sigma models on families of non-compact Calabi-Yau geometries, beginning with the resolved conifold and ALE spaces. Using the coset construction and Kazama-Suzuki formalism for non-compact cosets (e.g., SL(2,R)/U(1)), we will compute the (a,c) and (c,c) rings by classifying normalizable and delta-function-normalizable chiral primaries. Spectral flow will be implemented via the standard automorphism of the N=2 algebra, and we will track how boundary conditions at the non-compact end modify the flow orbits. We will cross-check ring relations against known results from topological string amplitudes on local Calabi-Yau threefolds and verify consistency with the elliptic genus computed via localization.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic classification of chiral ring structures for non-compact N=2 target spaces, filling a gap between the well-understood compact case and the increasingly important non-compact geometries in modern string theory. The results will directly inform Swampland conjectures by clarifying which effective field theory data is captured by protected operators in non-compact settings, and will yield new topological invariants for local Calabi-Yau geometries.
proposals.novelty: Unlike prior work that treats non-compact models case-by-case, our approach provides a unified framework linking boundary data at asymptotic infinity to the algebraic structure of the chiral ring, revealing how spectral flow orbits truncate or extend depending on the asymptotic geometry—a phenomenon with no compact analogue.

proposals.title: Chiral Ring Structure as a Precision Diagnostic for Two-Dimensional N=(2,2) Dualities and Swampland Constraints
proposals.motivation: Dualities between two-dimensional N=(2,2) supersymmetric theories have been proposed on the basis of matching central charges and elliptic genera, but these coarse invariants cannot distinguish theories that differ at the level of their operator algebras. Chiral rings encode the full structure of protected BPS operators and their OPE coefficients, providing a far more stringent consistency check. Given Vafa's program connecting the string landscape to swampland constraints, establishing or falsifying proposed 2d dualities at the chiral ring level would clarify which low-dimensional effective theories genuinely descend from consistent UV completions.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that exact chiral ring matching—including all ring relations, quantum deformations, and spectral sequence structure—can sharply discriminate between genuinely dual N=(2,2) theories and accidental agreements, and that failures of matching signal swampland obstructions to UV completion.
proposals.method: We will compute the chiral rings of candidate dual pairs by three complementary techniques. First, we use localization on the Coulomb branch to derive the twisted chiral ring relations for gauged linear sigma models (GLSMs) on both sides of proposed dualities, systematically including quantum corrections via the effective twisted superpotential. Second, we employ the half-twisted A/B-model topological field theory formulation to extract the full OPE algebra of chiral primary operators and compare ring presentations as graded polynomial quotient rings. Third, for theories with known Calabi-Yau target spaces, we cross-check against the mathematical computation of the quantum cohomology ring using Gromov-Witten invariants at genus zero. We organize the comparison using a spectral sequence that tracks how classical ring relations deform order by order in the FI-parameter expansion, enabling a systematic identification of where mismatches first appear.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic chiral ring comparison across a broad class of proposed 2d dualities, going well beyond existing checks based on partition functions and anomaly matching. It will produce a catalog of confirmed and falsified duality pairs, and connect failures of chiral ring matching to Vafa's swampland program by interpreting them as obstructions to consistent gravitational coupling. The spectral sequence framework developed here will serve as a reusable diagnostic tool for future duality proposals.
proposals.novelty: While chiral rings have been computed for individual 2d theories, no systematic program has used full ring-level matching—including quantum deformations and spectral sequence organization—as a precision duality test. Connecting chiral ring failures to swampland criteria is new and leverages Vafa's unique perspective bridging formal string consistency with concrete field-theoretic data.

proposals.title: Anti-Chiral Rings and Mirror Symmetry: A Dual Structure for Topological Field Theory Correlators
proposals.motivation: Mirror symmetry exchanges complex structure and Kähler moduli of Calabi-Yau manifolds, mapping the chiral ring of one theory to structures on its mirror. While the chiral ring of the (c,c) sector in N=(2,2) superconformal field theories is extensively studied, the anti-chiral ring arising from the (a,c) sector and its precise algebraic role under mirror symmetry remain underexplored. A systematic treatment of this dual ring would sharpen our understanding of mirror symmetry at the level of operator algebras and could yield new computational tools for topological string amplitudes.
proposals.hypothesis: The anti-chiral ring of an N=(2,2) SCFT carries an independent algebraic structure—dual to but not merely the conjugate of the chiral ring—and mirror symmetry acts as a canonical isomorphism exchanging these two rings, inducing non-trivial identities among topological correlators and flat coordinates on the enlarged moduli space.
proposals.method: We will construct the anti-chiral ring explicitly for Calabi-Yau sigma models by analyzing the (a,c) cohomology using spectral sequences adapted from the Hodge filtration on the target space. Topological field theory techniques, specifically topological anti-twisting of the B-model, will be employed to compute genus-zero correlators in the anti-chiral sector and compare them with A-model chiral ring correlators on the mirror manifold. We will verify the proposed ring isomorphism for known mirror pairs such as the quintic and its Greene-Plesser mirror using period integral calculations and the tt* equations of Cecotti and Vafa. Finally, we will examine how the Swampland distance conjecture constrains the asymptotic behavior of anti-chiral ring structure constants near singular loci in moduli space.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic algebraic characterization of the anti-chiral ring as a standalone structure in 2d SCFTs and establish its precise transformation under mirror symmetry. It will produce new identities relating topological correlators across dual sectors, potentially simplifying higher-genus amplitude computations. The connection to Swampland constraints will link this algebraic structure to broader questions in the string landscape program pioneered by Vafa.
proposals.novelty: Previous treatments subsume the anti-chiral ring as a conjugate of the chiral ring without independent analysis. Our approach treats it as a first-class algebraic object, uses topological anti-twisting to define its correlators intrinsically, and connects the resulting mirror map to Swampland distance conjectures—a combination not previously explored.

proposals.title: Double Scaling Limits of Calabi-Yau Geometries and a Complete Classification of (p,q) Minimal Model Universality Classes
proposals.motivation: Minimal models form the backbone of two-dimensional conformal field theory and appear as universal building blocks in string compactifications, yet the precise geometric origin of each (p,q) minimal model from Calabi-Yau moduli space remains incompletely mapped. Vafa and collaborators have long established deep connections between topological string theory on local Calabi-Yau threefolds and matrix models whose double scaling limits recover minimal model CFTs, but a systematic classification of which parameter tunings yield which universality classes is still lacking. Completing this classification would illuminate the landscape of consistent string vacua and sharpen the boundary between the swampland and the landscape.
proposals.hypothesis: Every unitary (p,q) minimal model universality class can be realized as a specific double scaling limit of the B-model topological string on a local Calabi-Yau geometry defined by a spectral curve of the form y² = W'(x)², and the full set of such limits is governed by the singularity structure of the spectral curve encoded in ADE-type degenerations.
proposals.method: We begin by parameterizing families of local Calabi-Yau threefolds via their spectral curves W'(x) = 0, systematically cataloguing all possible confluence patterns of critical points as moduli are tuned. For each degeneration pattern, we perform the double scaling limit of the associated matrix model free energy using the loop equation recursion of Eynard-Orantin topological recursion, extracting the central charge and operator content to identify the resulting (p,q) minimal model. We cross-check each identification by computing the genus-zero and genus-one free energies and matching against known minimal model partition functions. Finally, we organize the results into a classification table indexed by the ADE singularity type of the spectral curve, proving completeness by exhausting all allowed degeneration types consistent with Calabi-Yau geometry constraints.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first complete dictionary between double scaling limits of Calabi-Yau spectral curves and (p,q) minimal model universality classes, giving string theorists a precise geometric handle on each minimal model sector. The classification will also constrain which minimal model sectors can appear in flux compactifications, directly informing swampland program conjectures about the finiteness and structure of the string landscape.
proposals.novelty: While individual double scaling limits have been studied case by case, no prior work has systematically exhausted all possible parameter tunings and proven that the resulting classification is complete. Our approach combines Eynard-Orantin topological recursion with ADE singularity theory to turn an ad hoc enumeration into a rigorous, finite classification problem.

proposals.title: Toda Lattice Constraints on Exact Prepotentials and the Swampland Program
proposals.motivation: The exact prepotential governing gauge couplings in N=2 supersymmetric theories encodes deep non-perturbative physics, yet systematic methods for constraining its form across the string landscape remain underdeveloped. Integrable hierarchies such as the Toda lattice and KP hierarchy have long appeared in matrix model descriptions of gauge theories, but their implications for Swampland consistency conditions on gauge couplings have not been explored. Connecting these mathematical structures to Vafa's Swampland program could yield new universal constraints on which effective field theories admit UV completion.
proposals.hypothesis: The Toda and KP integrable hierarchy structures underlying the Seiberg-Witten prepotential impose quantitative constraints on gauge couplings that are equivalent to, or stronger than, known Swampland conjectures such as the Weak Gravity Conjecture and the Distance Conjecture in gauge coupling moduli space.
proposals.method: We begin by reformulating the Seiberg-Witten prepotential for classical gauge groups as a tau-function of the Toda lattice hierarchy, following the Marshakov-Mironov-Morozov framework, and extend this to exceptional groups using folding techniques on Dynkin diagrams. We then derive the discrete Hirota bilinear relations satisfied by these tau-functions and translate them into explicit inequalities and recursion relations among the higher-derivative gauge couplings. These relations will be compared term-by-term against the constraints predicted by the Weak Gravity Conjecture for gauge forces and the Distance Conjecture near weak-coupling limits. Finally, we embed the analysis into Type IIB string compactifications on Calabi-Yau threefolds where the prepotential receives worldsheet instanton corrections, testing whether the integrable hierarchy structure survives and yields new Swampland bounds on instanton-corrected couplings.
proposals.contribution: This work would establish a precise mathematical bridge between integrable systems and the Swampland program, providing new tools to classify consistent gauge sectors in quantum gravity. It would generate concrete, testable inequalities among gauge couplings that go beyond existing Swampland conjectures. The framework could also clarify why integrable structures appear so universally in supersymmetric gauge theories from a quantum gravity consistency perspective.
proposals.novelty: While both integrable hierarchies in gauge theory and Swampland constraints have been studied extensively in isolation, no prior work has systematically derived Swampland-type bounds from the Hirota bilinear equations of the Toda or KP hierarchy applied to the exact prepotential, nor examined whether integrability itself is a consequence of UV completability.

proposals.title: Geometric Engineering of Non-Supersymmetric Gauge Theories via Controlled SUSY-Breaking Calabi-Yau Deformations
proposals.motivation: Geometric engineering in string theory has traditionally relied on preserving N=2 supersymmetry through compactification on Calabi-Yau threefolds, limiting its applicability to realistic particle physics where supersymmetry must be broken. Vafa's Swampland program and recent work on non-supersymmetric string vacua suggest that consistent SUSY-breaking configurations are highly constrained yet physically essential. Developing a systematic geometric framework for engineering gauge theories with partial or soft supersymmetry breaking would bridge the gap between the elegant mathematical control of geometric engineering and phenomenologically viable low-energy physics.
proposals.hypothesis: Controlled deformations of local Calabi-Yau geometries—specifically non-Kähler resolutions and flux-induced obstructions to the holomorphic structure—can geometrically encode soft supersymmetry-breaking terms in the resulting four-dimensional gauge theory, while remaining consistent with Swampland constraints such as the de Sitter conjecture and the distance conjecture.
proposals.method: We begin by classifying non-Kähler deformations of the canonical local Calabi-Yau geometries used in geometric engineering (resolved ADE singularities fibered over curves), computing their effect on the low-energy effective action via dimensional reduction of the topological B-model with background fluxes. Specifically, we introduce anti-holomorphic flux perturbations parameterized by small deformation parameters and derive the resulting soft-breaking mass terms, A-terms, and B-terms using the generalized complex geometry framework of Hitchin and Gualtieri. We then apply Swampland consistency conditions—particularly the weak gravity conjecture and the non-SUSY AdS instability conjecture from Vafa's program—to constrain the allowed deformation space. Finally, we compute the modified Seiberg-Witten prepotential and check whether the resulting gauge couplings and mass spectra remain within the landscape rather than the swampland.
proposals.contribution: This work would establish the first systematic dictionary between geometric deformations of Calabi-Yau singularities and soft SUSY-breaking parameters in four-dimensional gauge theories, extending geometric engineering beyond its traditional N=2 limitation. It would provide concrete Swampland-consistent examples of non-supersymmetric string vacua with calculable low-energy physics, directly informing both formal string theory and BSM phenomenology.
proposals.novelty: While geometric engineering and SUSY-breaking have been studied independently, no existing framework systematically encodes soft-breaking terms as geometric data of deformed Calabi-Yau spaces subject to Swampland constraints. This proposal uniquely combines Vafa's Swampland program with the geometric engineering paradigm, turning consistency conditions into constructive tools for non-supersymmetric model building.

proposals.title: String-Scale Corrections to Gauge Dynamics from Geometric Engineering Beyond the Field Theory Limit
proposals.motivation: Geometric engineering of gauge theories via type II string compactifications on local Calabi-Yau threefolds has traditionally been studied in the field theory limit, where the string scale decouples and only the low-energy gauge dynamics survives. However, this decoupling discards a tower of stringy corrections that encode finite-coupling, UV-complete information about the gauge theory. Understanding these corrections is essential for connecting Swampland constraints and the string landscape to concrete gauge-theoretic predictions, a program central to Vafa's recent work on the Elementary Constituents Conjecture and quasicrystalline landscapes.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that retaining finite string-scale corrections in geometric engineering—by working at finite base Kähler moduli rather than taking the decompactification limit—yields a calculable dual description of strongly-coupled gauge dynamics, providing an explicit map between string-scale geometry and non-perturbative field theory data such as exact prepotentials and BPS spectra beyond the Seiberg-Witten approximation.
proposals.method: We will study type IIA compactifications on local Calabi-Yau threefolds engineered to produce N=2 gauge theories in four dimensions, systematically expanding the topological string free energy at finite base size using the refined topological vertex and modular bootstrap techniques. Specifically, we compute the genus-zero and genus-one topological string amplitudes on geometries engineering SU(2) and SU(3) gauge theories, retaining all orders in the base Kähler parameter that are normally sent to infinity. We then extract the corrected prepotential and BPS degeneracies via Gopakumar-Vafa invariants and compare them against known Seiberg-Witten results and their instanton corrections. Finally, we analyze how these finite-coupling corrections respect or constrain Swampland bounds, particularly the Distance Conjecture and the tower of states emerging at infinite-distance limits in moduli space.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic computation of string-scale corrections to geometrically engineered gauge theories at finite coupling, bridging a gap between exact field-theoretic results and full string-theoretic amplitudes. It will furnish concrete examples testing whether Swampland distance conjectures can be sharpened using exact topological string data. The results will inform the broader program of constraining the effective field theory landscape from UV completeness.
proposals.novelty: While geometric engineering and topological string computations are well-established, the deliberate study of the regime away from the field theory limit—treating the base size as a physical parameter encoding stringy corrections—has not been systematically pursued. This inverts the usual logic: rather than extracting field theory from strings, we use the known field theory as an anchor to decode genuinely stringy physics at finite coupling.

proposals.title: Non-Perturbative Instanton Corrections to Vacuum Transitions in the Quasicrystalline String Landscape
proposals.motivation: The string landscape contains a vast number of metastable vacua connected by transitions whose rates determine cosmological evolution. Vafa's recent work on quasicrystalline structure in the string landscape identifies geometric patterns governing vacuum connectivity, but this analysis relies on perturbative moduli space geometry and neglects non-perturbative effects. Worldsheet instantons and brane instantons are known to drastically modify effective potentials and transition amplitudes, potentially reshaping the vacuum transition network in ways that affect both the Swampland program and cosmological predictions.
proposals.hypothesis: Worldsheet and brane instanton corrections systematically modify the vacuum transition graph identified in the quasicrystalline string landscape, breaking certain geometric symmetries while preserving or enhancing quasicrystalline order through non-perturbative selection rules dictated by BPS charge conservation and Swampland constraints.
proposals.method: We compute worldsheet instanton contributions to the superpotential in explicit Calabi-Yau compactifications that realize sectors of the quasicrystalline landscape, using mirror symmetry and Gromov-Witten invariants to extract exact prepotentials. For brane instanton corrections, we classify Euclidean D-brane configurations wrapping calibrated cycles and evaluate their contributions to vacuum transition amplitudes via the Coleman-De Luccia formalism augmented with stringy prefactors. We systematically compare the corrected transition rates against the perturbative geometric predictions, constructing modified adjacency matrices for the vacuum network. Finally, we apply Swampland distance and weak gravity conjecture constraints to determine which instanton-corrected transitions remain consistent with quantum gravity requirements.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic analysis of how non-perturbative string corrections reshape vacuum transition networks in landscapes with quasicrystalline structure. It establishes concrete criteria for when perturbative geometric analyses of the landscape are reliable versus when instanton effects qualitatively alter the connectivity, directly informing the Swampland program and the viability of specific cosmological transition chains.
proposals.novelty: While instanton corrections to individual string vacua are well-studied, their collective effect on the transition graph topology of a structured landscape has not been analyzed. Combining Vafa's quasicrystalline landscape geometry with exact non-perturbative computations reveals whether the emergent order is a robust feature of string theory or an artifact of the perturbative approximation.

proposals.title: Systematic Classification of (0,4) Fivebranes in Heterotic String Compactifications
proposals.motivation: Fivebranes carrying (0,4) worldvolume supersymmetry play a central role in heterotic string dualities and geometric transitions, yet only isolated examples have been constructed in the literature. A complete classification would illuminate the landscape of non-perturbative heterotic vacua and sharpen Swampland constraints on allowed gauge symmetry breaking patterns. Given Vafa's program connecting string landscape structure to consistency conditions, this classification is a natural next step toward understanding the full heterotic moduli space.
proposals.hypothesis: All (0,4) fivebranes in heterotic strings can be systematically classified by their embedding data—specifically by the topology of the normal bundle, the instanton number partitioning across gauge factors, and discrete holonomy data—yielding a finite number of families that are exhaustively enumerable for a given compactification manifold.
proposals.method: We employ an algebraic-geometric approach, classifying (0,4) fivebranes by their sheaf-theoretic data on the internal Calabi-Yau threefold. First, we enumerate admissible spectral covers and their deformations for both E8 factors, cataloguing how instanton numbers can be distributed to satisfy the Bianchi identity with fivebrane source terms. Second, we use anomaly inflow analysis on the fivebrane worldvolume to constrain the allowed (0,4) multiplet content, cross-checking with heterotic-F-theory duality maps where the fivebranes correspond to specific singular fiber degenerations. Third, we systematically scan K3-fibered Calabi-Yau threefolds using toric geometry databases, identifying all topologically distinct fivebrane wrappings and their moduli dimensions. Finally, we apply Swampland consistency conditions—particularly the absence of global symmetries and completeness of the charge spectrum—to prune unphysical branches.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first complete taxonomy of (0,4) fivebranes in heterotic compactifications, organized by topological and gauge-theoretic invariants. It would establish new Swampland-type bounds on the number and type of fivebranes allowed in consistent heterotic vacua. The classification would also yield concrete predictions for dual F-theory geometries, testable via explicit construction of elliptic fibrations with matching singular fibers.
proposals.novelty: Previous studies examined (0,4) fivebranes only in the context of specific transitions between Calabi-Yau manifolds, treating them as intermediary objects rather than subjects of independent classification. Our approach inverts this perspective, using the fivebranes themselves as the organizing principle and leveraging modern Swampland criteria to achieve finiteness results that were previously inaccessible.

proposals.title: A Systematic Classification of Vacuum Transitions in the N=1 Six-Dimensional F-Theory Landscape
proposals.motivation: Six-dimensional N=1 supergravity theories occupy a critical intermediate position in the string landscape, constrained enough by anomaly cancellation to permit systematic enumeration yet rich enough to exhibit intricate vacuum structure. Recent constructions by Vafa and collaborators have dramatically expanded the catalog of consistent N=1 d=6 F-theory vacua through novel base geometries and gauge algebra configurations. A complete map of transitions among these vacua is essential for understanding the connected components of the string landscape, testing Swampland conjectures on vacuum connectivity, and identifying possible cosmological implications of vacuum decay in higher-dimensional compactifications.
proposals.hypothesis: The full web of transitions among N=1 six-dimensional F-theory vacua forms a connected graph when tensor branch deformations, Higgsing chains, and extremal transitions of the compactification base are systematically combined, and this connectivity can be established by an explicit enumeration leveraging anomaly cancellation constraints and the classification of allowed base surfaces.
proposals.method: We begin by cataloging all known consistent N=1 d=6 F-theory models built over compact base surfaces, encoding each vacuum by its tensor multiplet count, gauge algebra, and hypermultiplet spectrum. We then systematically enumerate three classes of transitions: (1) Higgsing chains that reduce gauge symmetry via hypermultiplet vacuum expectation values, tracked through representation-theoretic branching rules; (2) tensor branch flows corresponding to blow-ups and blow-downs of the base surface, analyzed via intersection theory on rational surfaces; (3) extremal transitions—simultaneous contractions and complex structure deformations—identified through toric and non-toric geometric techniques. Each candidate transition is checked against gravitational and gauge anomaly cancellation at both endpoints. The resulting directed graph is analyzed for connected components, diameter, and hub structure using standard graph-theoretic algorithms.
proposals.contribution: This work will produce the first complete transition graph of the six-dimensional N=1 F-theory landscape, providing a concrete testing ground for the Swampland Distance Conjecture and the Cobordism Conjecture applied to higher-dimensional vacua. It will identify whether isolated islands of vacua exist or whether all models are connected by physical transitions, directly informing the ongoing debate on landscape versus swampland boundaries. The explicit catalog of transition paths will also serve as a reference for future dimensional reduction studies linking six-dimensional physics to four-dimensional phenomenology.
proposals.novelty: While prior work has mapped Higgsing trees for six-dimensional N=1 theories and classified allowed base geometries separately, no systematic study has combined all three transition mechanisms—Higgsing, tensor branch flows, and extremal transitions—into a unified connectivity graph. The integration of these distinct geometric and field-theoretic operations into a single framework, together with the recently enlarged set of vacua from quasicrystalline and novel base constructions, makes this a non-trivial extension that could reveal unexpected connections invisible to any single transition mechanism alone.

proposals.title: Persistent Homology of String Moduli Spaces: Computational Detection of Non-Trivial Cycles in Calabi-Yau Compactification Landscapes
proposals.motivation: The topology of string moduli spaces encodes critical information about vacuum selection, dualities, and the swampland program, yet systematic computational tools for probing their global topological structure remain underdeveloped. Vafa's recent work on quasicrystalline landscapes and the swampland conjectures highlights the importance of understanding the geometric and topological organization of the string landscape. Persistent homology, a cornerstone of topological data analysis (TDA), offers a principled, scale-invariant method to detect and characterize topological features that traditional algebraic geometry approaches may miss.
proposals.hypothesis: Non-trivial persistent homological features (long-lived cycles in Betti number filtrations) of sampled moduli spaces of Calabi-Yau compactifications encode physically meaningful structures, including boundaries of the swampland, duality walls, and phase transition loci, that correlate with known constraints from the Weak Gravity Conjecture and the Distance Conjecture.
proposals.method: We construct point-cloud approximations of moduli spaces for well-studied Calabi-Yau threefolds and fourfolds by sampling complex structure and Kähler moduli using numerical techniques from the CYTools and CICY databases. We then compute persistent homology via Vietoris-Rips and alpha complexes using the GUDHI and Ripser libraries, extracting persistence diagrams and barcodes across multiple filtration scales. Topological features with anomalously long persistence lifetimes are catalogued and cross-referenced against known geometric loci such as conifold points, orbifold singularities, and infinite-distance limits relevant to the Distance Conjecture. We further apply persistence landscapes and vectorization techniques to enable statistical comparison across different compactification families and dimensionalities.
proposals.contribution: This work establishes the first systematic application of TDA to string moduli spaces, providing a computational toolkit for detecting topological signatures that complement analytic swampland criteria. It will produce a publicly available dataset of persistence diagrams for standard Calabi-Yau moduli spaces, enabling future data-driven investigations of the landscape. The results may reveal previously unrecognized topological obstructions or connectivity structures relevant to vacuum selection and cosmological transitions.
proposals.novelty: While persistent homology has been applied in condensed matter and lattice QCD, its application to string moduli spaces is entirely unexplored, bridging TDA with the swampland program in a non-obvious way. The approach is distinctive in treating moduli spaces as data objects rather than purely algebraic-geometric constructs, enabling discovery of features invisible to traditional cohomological methods.

proposals.title: Boundary Conditions on Non-Compact Calabi-Yau Four-Folds and Local Singularity Physics with Finite Cutoff Effects in M-Theory
proposals.motivation: Compactifications of M-theory and F-theory on Calabi-Yau four-folds have been instrumental in constructing four-dimensional vacua with realistic gauge symmetries and matter content, yet most constructions assume compact geometries that obscure local singularity physics. Non-compact four-folds naturally arise in local model building, but the absence of systematic boundary conditions leaves the effective theory incomplete, with divergent moduli spaces and uncontrolled asymptotic behavior. Introducing well-defined boundary conditions would enable a rigorous treatment of finite cutoff effects, connecting local singularity engineering to the Swampland program and Vafa's recent work on M-theory-like vacua in four dimensions.
proposals.hypothesis: Non-compact Calabi-Yau four-folds equipped with appropriate asymptotic boundary conditions yield a finite-dimensional moduli space whose effective physics captures local singularity data—such as gauge symmetry enhancement and matter localization—while encoding UV cutoff effects that are consistent with Swampland constraints on the landscape of four-dimensional vacua.
proposals.method: We construct explicit non-compact Calabi-Yau four-fold geometries as neighborhoods of singular loci in known compact examples, retaining ADE-type singularities relevant for gauge symmetry engineering. Asymptotic boundary conditions are imposed by specifying the behavior of the Kähler and holomorphic four-form at the non-compact boundary, analogous to the approach used for non-compact Calabi-Yau three-folds in geometric engineering. We compute the resulting finite-dimensional moduli space using techniques from relative cohomology and boundary-valued Hodge theory, and derive the effective three-dimensional and four-dimensional supergravity by dimensional reduction of eleven-dimensional supergravity on these bounded geometries. The finite cutoff scale introduced by the boundary is matched against Swampland distance and tower conjectures to check consistency with quantum gravity constraints.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic framework for imposing boundary conditions on non-compact Calabi-Yau four-folds in the context of M-theory and F-theory compactifications, yielding a controlled local model-building toolkit with built-in UV sensitivity. It establishes a concrete link between local singularity physics and global Swampland constraints, offering new tests of the Elementary Constituents Conjecture and the landscape structure explored in Vafa's quasicrystalline string landscape program.
proposals.novelty: While non-compact Calabi-Yau three-folds are standard in geometric engineering of five-dimensional and four-dimensional gauge theories, the extension to four-folds with systematic boundary conditions is unexplored and requires new cohomological and supergravity techniques. The interplay between finite cutoff effects from the boundary and Swampland bounds provides a novel quantitative bridge between local model building and global consistency conditions that has not been previously formulated.

proposals.title: Reconstructing Calabi-Yau Four-Fold Geometries from Two-Dimensional Superconformal Field Theories via Algebraic Invariants and Machine Learning
proposals.motivation: The geometric engineering program in string theory establishes deep correspondences between singular Calabi-Yau geometries and superconformal field theories, yet the inverse problem—recovering the geometry from CFT data—remains largely unsolved. Solving this inverse map would provide a powerful new tool for navigating the string landscape and connecting bottom-up model building with top-down geometric constructions. This is especially urgent given Vafa's Swampland program, where identifying which effective theories admit UV completions requires understanding the precise geometric origins of low-dimensional SCFTs.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the chiral algebra, central charge, and BPS spectrum of a two-dimensional (0,2) or (2,2) SCFT, combined with its elliptic genus and anomaly polynomial, contain sufficient information to uniquely reconstruct (up to discrete ambiguities) the local Calabi-Yau four-fold singularity and G-flux configuration that engineers the theory via M-theory or F-theory compactification.
proposals.method: We will systematically classify families of Calabi-Yau four-fold singularities—including compound Du Val and terminal singularities—and compute their associated 2d SCFT data (central charges, flavor symmetry algebras, elliptic genera) using anomaly inflow and localization techniques developed by Benini, Bobev, and collaborators. From this forward map we construct a structured database pairing geometric data (toric diagrams, intersection numbers, flux quanta) with CFT observables. We then train graph neural networks on this database, where the input graph encodes the BPS quiver and flavor symmetry structure of the CFT and the output predicts the combinatorial data of the singularity resolution. Finally, we validate candidate geometries by checking consistency conditions from the Swampland conjectures, including the absence of global symmetries and the Weak Gravity Conjecture applied to the BPS tower.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic algorithm for solving the inverse geometric engineering problem for 2d SCFTs from Calabi-Yau four-folds, yielding both a curated database and a trained inference tool. It will sharpen the boundary between the landscape and the Swampland in two dimensions and offer concrete new examples connecting Vafa's cobordism and finiteness conjectures to computable CFT data.
proposals.novelty: While forward maps from geometry to CFT are well-studied, no systematic inverse reconstruction exists for the four-fold case. Combining Swampland consistency filters with machine-learned geometric inference on BPS quiver data is a genuinely new approach that leverages recent advances in both mathematical string theory and AI-for-physics.

proposals.title: Instanton Partition Functions and the Microscopic OSV Conjecture: A Gauge-Theoretic Derivation of Black Hole Entropy from Topological String Amplitudes
proposals.motivation: The OSV conjecture relating black hole entropy to the square of the topological string partition function has been established primarily from the macroscopic supergravity side, leaving the microscopic gauge-theoretic derivation incomplete. A direct derivation from instanton counting would provide a first-principles verification of the conjecture and illuminate the deep connection between gauge theory, black holes, and topological strings. Given Vafa's foundational contributions to both topological string theory and the OSV relation, closing this gap represents a natural and high-impact direction.
proposals.hypothesis: The Nekrasov instanton partition function for N=2 gauge theories arising on D-branes wrapping cycles in Calabi-Yau compactifications, when evaluated in the self-dual Omega-background limit, reproduces the topological string partition function and thereby provides a microscopic gauge-theoretic derivation of the OSV relation for BPS black holes.
proposals.method: We begin by constructing the relevant N=2 gauge theory on the worldvolume of D4-branes wrapping divisors in a Calabi-Yau threefold, identifying the instanton moduli space with the moduli of ideal sheaves. We then compute the Nekrasov partition function using equivariant localization on the instanton moduli space, taking the self-dual limit epsilon_1 = -epsilon_2 where the partition function reduces to the genus expansion of the topological string. The key technical step involves matching the instanton sum with the Gopakumar-Vafa invariants that encode the topological string amplitudes, and then demonstrating that the resulting mixed partition function satisfies the attractor mechanism to yield the correct black hole degeneracies. We employ wall-crossing formulae of Kontsevich-Soibelman type to track BPS state counting across chambers in moduli space.
proposals.contribution: This work would provide the first complete microscopic derivation of the OSV conjecture from gauge-theoretic first principles, bridging the gap between Nekrasov's instanton calculus and black hole microstate counting. It would establish a concrete computational dictionary between instanton partition functions and topological string amplitudes in the black hole context, and potentially reveal new BPS invariants accessible only from the gauge theory side.
proposals.novelty: While prior work has connected Nekrasov partition functions to topological strings in flat space or local Calabi-Yau geometries, no complete treatment has carried this through the attractor mechanism to derive the full OSV relation microscopically. Our approach of combining equivariant instanton calculus with wall-crossing formulae in the compact Calabi-Yau setting is a non-trivial synthesis that goes beyond existing partial results.

proposals.title: Universal Weak Gravity Bounds for p-Form Gauge Fields in D-Dimensional String Compactifications
proposals.motivation: The Weak Gravity Conjecture (WGC), proposed by Arkani-Hamed, Motl, Nicolis, and Vafa in 2006, asserts that gravity must be the weakest force, requiring the existence of states whose charge-to-mass ratio exceeds that of extremal black holes. While extensively studied for 1-form gauge fields, its generalization to higher p-form gauge fields in arbitrary spacetime dimension D remains incomplete, despite p-forms being ubiquitous in string and M-theory compactifications. A systematic treatment would sharpen the Swampland program's ability to constrain effective field theories and illuminate the structure of the string landscape.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that for every p-form gauge field in a D-dimensional theory of quantum gravity, there exists a charged (p-1)-brane whose tension-to-charge ratio is bounded above by a universal function of p, D, and the gravitational coupling, and that this bound is saturated by BPS states in supersymmetric compactifications.
proposals.method: We will derive the extremality bound for charged black branes coupled to p-form gauge fields in D dimensions by analyzing the Einstein-p-form action and extracting the critical tension-to-charge ratio from the corresponding black brane solutions. We then systematically test the conjectured bound against known BPS and non-BPS brane spectra in type II, heterotic, and M-theory compactifications on Calabi-Yau manifolds, tori, and orbifolds, cataloging cases where the bound is satisfied or saturated. Dimensional reduction techniques will be employed to relate higher-form WGC bounds in D dimensions to lower-form bounds in reduced dimensions, establishing consistency under compactification. Finally, we examine implications for the lattice and sublattice versions of the WGC, determining whether the convex hull condition on the charge lattice extends naturally to brane charge lattices.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first comprehensive, dimension- and form-degree-general statement of the Weak Gravity Conjecture with precise numerical bounds derived from black brane physics. It will furnish new Swampland constraints on effective field theories containing higher-form gauge fields, directly applicable to flux compactifications central to string phenomenology. The results will also clarify the interplay between the WGC and other Swampland conjectures, such as the Distance Conjecture, in the context of higher-form symmetries.
proposals.novelty: While partial results exist for specific values of p and D, no unified framework treats arbitrary p-form fields across all dimensions with explicit extremality bounds. Our approach of systematically cross-checking against the full brane spectrum of known string and M-theory vacua, combined with dimensional reduction consistency checks, goes beyond case-by-case analyses and reveals universal structural patterns invisible in restricted settings.

proposals.title: The Weak Gravity Conjecture for Non-Abelian Gauge Symmetries: Bounds from String Compactifications and Lattice Constraints
proposals.motivation: The Weak Gravity Conjecture (WGC) has been extensively studied for U(1) gauge fields, providing powerful constraints on effective field theories consistent with quantum gravity. However, realistic string compactifications and the Standard Model itself feature non-abelian gauge groups such as SU(2) and SU(3), where the appropriate generalization of the WGC remains poorly understood. Formulating precise non-abelian gravity bounds would significantly sharpen the Swampland program and constrain phenomenologically relevant theories.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that the WGC admits a natural non-abelian generalization in which superextremal states must exist in specific representations of the gauge group, with the bound determined by the quadratic Casimir of the representation rather than the simple charge-to-mass ratio, and that this generalized bound is universally satisfied in consistent string theory vacua.
proposals.method: We will systematically study string compactifications with non-abelian gauge symmetry, including heterotic compactifications on Calabi-Yau threefolds and Type IIA/IIB orientifolds with D-brane stacks yielding SU(N) gauge groups. For each class of vacua, we will catalog the spectrum of BPS and near-BPS states, computing their masses and representations under the non-abelian gauge group. We will formulate candidate non-abelian WGC bounds using group-theoretic quantities—primarily the quadratic Casimir C_2(R), the Dynkin index, and the dimension of the representation—and test these bounds against the explicit spectra. Additionally, we will examine the convex hull condition generalized to the weight lattice of the non-abelian group, checking whether the convex hull of charge-to-mass vectors in the root space satisfies the appropriate extremality condition.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic formulation and string-theoretic verification of a non-abelian Weak Gravity Conjecture, extending a cornerstone Swampland constraint beyond the abelian case. The results will yield concrete representation-theoretic bounds applicable to SU(N) gauge theories arising from string compactifications, with direct implications for constraining beyond-the-Standard-Model physics from quantum gravity consistency. The framework will also clarify the relationship between the non-abelian WGC and existing conjectures such as the tower and sublattice versions of the WGC.
proposals.novelty: While prior work on the WGC has focused almost exclusively on U(1) factors or treated non-abelian groups only through their Cartan subalgebra, our approach directly addresses the full non-abelian structure by leveraging representation-theoretic data and the weight lattice geometry, providing bounds that cannot be reduced to multiple abelian constraints and that capture genuinely non-abelian phenomena such as confinement thresholds and representation-dependent extremality.

proposals.title: The Strong Gravity Conjecture: Lower Bounds on Gravitational Coupling from Swampland Consistency Conditions
proposals.motivation: The Swampland program, largely pioneered by Vafa and collaborators, has established powerful upper bounds on effective field theory parameters consistent with quantum gravity, most notably through the Weak Gravity Conjecture. However, the inverse question — whether quantum gravity also imposes a minimum gravitational coupling strength relative to other forces — remains unexplored. Establishing such a lower bound would complete the logical structure of Swampland constraints and potentially constrain the landscape of string vacua from a new direction.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that consistency conditions from quantum gravity, including the absence of stable remnants, completeness of the charge spectrum, and cobordism constraints, collectively impose a lower bound on the effective gravitational coupling in any dimension, which we term the Strong Gravity Conjecture. This bound is expected to be saturated by extremal configurations in string compactifications with maximal supersymmetry.
proposals.method: We will systematically derive constraints on the gravitational coupling by analyzing the interplay between black hole thermodynamics, species bounds, and the Distance Conjecture in moduli space. Concretely, we examine towers of light states arising at infinite-distance limits in Calabi-Yau compactifications and use the species scale framework of van de Heisteeg, Vafa, and Wiesner to bound Newton's constant from below. We will cross-check these bounds against known string vacua — including the quasicrystalline landscape constructions — by computing the effective Planck mass in explicit compactification geometries. Finally, we employ dimensional reduction from M-theory-like vacua in four dimensions to verify that the conjectured bound is preserved under compactification and duality transformations.
proposals.contribution: This work would establish a new Swampland conjecture that constrains the gravitational sector from below, complementing the existing Weak Gravity Conjecture. It provides a novel organizing principle for the string landscape by eliminating vacua with anomalously weak gravity. The resulting inequality, linking gravitational coupling to the spectrum of light states, offers concrete predictions testable against future compactification constructions.
proposals.novelty: While existing Swampland conjectures constrain gauge couplings and scalar potentials, no systematic lower bound on gravitational strength has been proposed. By dualizing the logic of the Weak Gravity Conjecture through the species scale formalism and infinite-distance tower analysis, this approach is non-obvious and opens an entirely new axis of Swampland constraints.

proposals.title: Higher-Order Quantum Corrections to Toda Superpotentials via Topological String Amplitudes and the Swampland Program
proposals.motivation: The Landau-Ginzburg Toda superpotential encodes essential non-perturbative data of mirror dual Calabi-Yau geometries, yet systematic computation of quantum corrections beyond leading order remains incomplete for most compact examples. Understanding these corrections is critical for precision tests of mirror symmetry and for constraining the string landscape, connecting directly to Vafa's Swampland program where the exact form of effective superpotentials determines which low-energy theories admit consistent UV completions. A controlled higher-order expansion would sharpen constraints from the Swampland Distance Conjecture and the de Sitter conjecture on flux vacua.
proposals.hypothesis: Subleading quantum corrections to the Toda superpotential, computed via genus-zero and genus-one topological string amplitudes on toric Calabi-Yau threefolds, introduce systematic shifts to the critical locus structure that tighten Swampland bounds on scalar field ranges and vacuum stability in the corresponding four-dimensional effective theories.
proposals.method: We employ the B-model topological string framework, using the extended holomorphic anomaly equations of Bershadsky-Cecotti-Ooguri-Vafa to compute genus-zero and genus-one free energies on a family of toric Calabi-Yau threefolds with known Toda-type mirrors. The mirror map is inverted order-by-order in the complexified Kähler moduli using Picard-Fuchs differential equations, and instanton corrections are extracted from the resulting expansion of the superpotential around large complex structure. We systematically classify corrections by worldsheet instanton degree and compare critical point shifts against numerical Gromov-Witten invariant databases. Finally, the corrected superpotentials are fed into the scalar potential of four-dimensional N=1 flux compactifications to quantify the impact on Swampland Distance Conjecture bounds.
proposals.contribution: This work will provide the first systematic catalog of subleading quantum corrections to Toda superpotentials for a representative class of toric Calabi-Yau mirrors, filling a computational gap in the mirror symmetry literature. The resulting corrected effective potentials will yield quantitative refinements to Swampland bounds, offering concrete predictions testable against future lattice or bootstrap calculations. The methodology establishes a reusable pipeline connecting topological string amplitudes to Swampland phenomenology.
proposals.novelty: While individual quantum corrections to mirror superpotentials have been studied case-by-case, no systematic treatment connects higher-order Toda corrections to Swampland constraints. By combining the BCOV holomorphic anomaly machinery with the Swampland program's consistency criteria, this proposal bridges two largely independent research streams in Vafa's own body of work, yielding constraints that neither approach produces alone.

proposals.title: Supersymmetric Localization of Vortex-Generated Superpotentials in Mirror Symmetry
proposals.motivation: Mirror symmetry derivations for two-dimensional N=(2,2) gauge theories rely on semiclassical evaluation of vortex contributions to the twisted superpotential, yet rigorous justification for the exactness of these saddle-point computations remains incomplete. Given Vafa's foundational contributions to mirror symmetry and the Swampland program's demand for precise effective potentials, establishing exact results via localization would strengthen both the mathematical foundations of mirror symmetry and its applications to the string landscape. A rigorous framework would also clarify which features of the superpotential are protected and which receive corrections, a question directly relevant to vacuum counting in the landscape.
proposals.hypothesis: The vortex-generated superpotential arising in the Hori-Vafa mirror derivation for two-dimensional N=(2,2) gauged linear sigma models can be computed exactly using supersymmetric localization on the Coulomb branch, and the localized path integral reproduces the known mirror map without semiclassical approximation, while revealing previously inaccessible non-perturbative correction terms at higher vortex number.
proposals.method: We apply the Pestun-style supersymmetric localization technique adapted to two-dimensional N=(2,2) gauge theories on S^2 and the Omega-background, building on the Benini-Zaffaroni and Doroud-Gomis-Le Floch-Lee partition function results. Concretely, we deform the path integral by a Q-exact term that localizes onto the vortex moduli space, then evaluate the one-loop determinants around each vortex saddle using index theorem methods on the moduli space of pointed vortices. We systematically classify the fixed-point loci under the localizing supercharge for U(N) gauge groups with fundamental and adjoint matter, computing the equivariant integral via Jeffrey-Kirwan residue prescriptions. The resulting exact partition function is then compared term-by-term with the mirror Landau-Ginzburg superpotential to extract corrections beyond the leading semiclassical vortex sum.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first complete localization proof that the Hori-Vafa mirror superpotential is exact for a broad class of gauged linear sigma models, removing reliance on semiclassical arguments. It identifies a systematic series of non-perturbative corrections from multi-vortex sectors that modify the effective superpotential at strong coupling, with direct implications for vacuum structure in Vafa's Swampland program. The framework also yields new mathematical identities relating equivariant vortex integrals to periods of mirror Calabi-Yau manifolds.
proposals.novelty: While localization has been used to compute S^2 partition functions, no prior work has systematically extracted the full vortex-generated superpotential including multi-vortex corrections and matched it to the mirror map at all orders. Our Jeffrey-Kirwan residue approach to the vortex moduli space fixed-point classification is new and resolves ambiguities in wall-crossing that previous Coulomb branch analyses left open.

proposals.title: Emergent Calabi-Yau Geometry from the Large-N Limit of Enlarged Gauge Symmetries in Mirror Constructions
proposals.motivation: Mirror symmetry exchanges complex structure and Kähler moduli of dual Calabi-Yau manifolds, yet its behavior under large-rank gauge enhancements remains largely unexplored. Vafa's recent work on quasicrystalline string landscapes and the Swampland program suggests that infinite-rank limits may reveal new geometric structures constrained by quantum gravity consistency conditions. Understanding how emergent geometry arises from large-N enlarged gauge symmetry could illuminate both the landscape of string vacua and non-perturbative dualities.
proposals.hypothesis: We hypothesize that taking the rank of an enlarged gauge group to infinity in the mirror construction produces a well-defined emergent Calabi-Yau geometry whose topological invariants are determined by large-N saddle-point equations, and that this emergent geometry satisfies Swampland distance conjectures with computable corrections in 1/N.
proposals.method: We begin by constructing explicit mirror pairs for Calabi-Yau threefolds with gauge symmetry enlarged by successive Higgsing chains of ADE type, parametrized by rank N. Using the Hori-Vafa mirror prescription for gauged linear sigma models, we derive the mirror superpotential at finite N and then analyze its saddle-point structure as N tends to infinity via large-N matrix model techniques. Specifically, we employ the Dijkgraaf-Vafa correspondence relating the superpotential to a matrix integral whose spectral curve defines the emergent geometry. We compute the resulting Seiberg-Witten-like curve and its periods, checking consistency with the Swampland distance conjecture by evaluating geodesic distances in the emergent moduli space at leading and subleading order in 1/N.
proposals.contribution: This work provides the first systematic study of mirror symmetry in the large-N limit of enlarged gauge groups, establishing whether emergent Calabi-Yau geometries arise and characterizing their topological properties. It connects the Swampland program to large-N gauge theory dynamics, offering new quantitative tests of distance conjectures. The results may also yield new infinite families of dual pairs relevant to flux compactifications.
proposals.novelty: While large-N limits are well-studied in gauge/gravity duality, applying them to mirror constructions with systematically enlarged gauge symmetry is novel. The combination of Hori-Vafa mirror maps with Dijkgraaf-Vafa matrix models in this context has not been explored, and the resulting constraints from Swampland conjectures on emergent large-N geometry are entirely new.

ideas.title: Equivariant M2 Localization via Type IIA/K3 Reduction
ideas.description: This direction uses M-theory/Type IIA duality to compactify the localized M2-brane problem onto backgrounds involving K3, where supersymmetric sectors are simpler and the relevant fixed loci can be described in terms of holomorphic curves, sheaf moduli, or topological sigma-model data. The key idea is to transport equivariant parameters and supercharge choices through the reduction so that the exact M2 path integral becomes a lower-dimensional K3 amplitude that can be evaluated and matched against established BPS and topological invariants. In this way, K3 is not just a toy model but a calibration space that identifies which localization saddle points survive the duality and how their measure factors should be organized. Success on K3 would provide a concrete blueprint for reconstructing exact M-theory amplitudes from reduced geometries and for extending localization beyond perturbative gauge-theory settings.

ideas.title: Extracting BPS invariants via persistent homology inspired by topological data analysis
ideas.description: This proposal adapts the core methodology of persistent homology from topological data analysis to the problem of rigorously extracting integer BPS invariants from topological string partition functions. In TDA, one constructs a filtration of topological spaces indexed by a scale parameter and computes homology at each level; features that persist across a wide range of scales correspond to genuine topological invariants (integer Betti numbers), while short-lived features are discarded as noise. Analogously, we propose constructing a filtration of the moduli space of a Calabi-Yau compactification parameterized by the string coupling or Kähler parameters. At each filtration level, one decomposes the partition function into polar and finite parts, tracking which BPS contributions persist as the parameters vary continuously. Contributions that survive across a broad range of the filtration correspond to true integer BPS degeneracies, while wall-crossing artifacts and multi-particle contributions appear as transient features that can be algorithmically identified and removed. The resulting 'persistence diagram' for BPS states would provide a systematic, mathematically rigorous method for reading off integer invariants, replacing case-by-case analyses with a unified computational framework. This approach also naturally interfaces with existing mathematical structures: the persistence module decomposition theorem (which guarantees a barcode representation) mirrors the expected decomposition of the partition function into contributions from individual BPS multiplets, and the stability theorem from TDA would translate into bounds on how BPS degeneracies change under small deformations of the Calabi-Yau geometry.

ideas.title: Seismic tomography methods for reconstructing Calabi-Yau geometry from partition function data
ideas.description: This idea adapts the methodology of seismic tomography — the premier solved inverse problem in geophysics — to the challenge of extracting Calabi-Yau geometry from topological string amplitudes. In seismic tomography, observed waveforms at Earth's surface are inverted to reconstruct the 3D internal structure of the planet, despite the problem being ill-posed and nonlinear. The analogy is precise: just as seismic waves encode the geometry of the medium they propagate through, the Gopakumar-Vafa invariants and free energy coefficients of the topological string partition function encode the geometric and topological data of the target Calabi-Yau manifold. The adapted approach proceeds in three stages. First, establish the 'forward model' — the explicit map from Calabi-Yau data (triple intersection numbers, second Chern class, periods of the holomorphic 3-form, and curve class structure) to the genus-expanded partition function, leveraging mirror symmetry and the holomorphic anomaly equations. Second, construct an iterative inversion scheme: begin with a reference Calabi-Yau (analogous to a reference Earth model), compute the predicted partition function, compare with the target data, and update the geometric parameters using gradient-based or adjoint methods analogous to tomographic back-projection. Regularization — via topological constraints (integrality of Hodge numbers, positivity of Kähler cone) — tames the ill-posedness, just as smoothness priors do in geophysics. Third, deploy Bayesian sampling over the space of candidate geometries to map out the posterior distribution, revealing whether the inverse map is unique or admits a finite discrete family of solutions. This framework transforms the abstract mathematical inverse problem into a concrete computational pipeline with well-understood convergence and uncertainty properties.

ideas.title: Precision finite-charge test of the OSV conjecture via exact degeneracy formulas in controlled string compactifications
ideas.description: We propose a systematic, falsifiable test of the OSV conjecture at finite charges by exploiting string compactifications where exact microscopic degeneracies are known in closed form, such as the STU model and one-parameter Calabi-Yau examples with dual heterotic descriptions. Using Rademacher-type expansions and Hardy-Ramanujan-type exact formulas for BPS state counts, we compute the microstate degeneracy at specific small-to-moderate charge values and compare against the OSV integral evaluated with all known perturbative and non-perturbative corrections to the prepotential and integration measure. This approach transforms the vague question of whether the OSV relation requires fundamental modification into a concrete numerical program with quantifiable discrepancies, enabling us to identify whether deviations follow a systematic pattern suggestive of a missing structural ingredient or converge to zero, thereby resolving the status of the conjecture beyond the asymptotic regime.
